name: Tim Burchett
subject: The deed is done – I am the new Chairman of the DOGE Committee.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
As my first order of business...
| FROM THE DESK OF THE NEW DOGE COMMITTEE CHAIRMAN RE: Nationwide DOGE Survey
---
My fellow Americans:
The deed is done - I am the new Chairman of the DOGE Committee.

[As my first order of business, I’m putting together my first-ever Nationwide DOGE Survey. And I’d be grateful to include your feedback.](http://links.right-red.com/u/click?_t=7f2cc7f35be547959fee68a54bdac0d9&_m=30a831b3ce0d407d85089aecb663f42f&_e=NOcyjFKAQiycVu94xNpbYyrjty7U4AmJHnKAQo_Vdiutq7LbGg4ziXCV3MGSjT-X0oVW7gbFlDG_hrKO6qc7LUWR64m-J8Lpdodswfe4A3-DnEJaUNRVeVVSXqqBu1gGXcq2M1s0dnGx82t7-wge_f4WiV7EoYdVc-qRt2JWLz6Im40a3wjQy_XKTr1PI1dmyR7MQWt5A8mKiWSeebFTz5Unms7svadsHBwHdpLFpIr3uihWho8boVTH2xuHfPoN4w-YifKB_PWm8tnBNW7mNjnEmvJDX8ip4y2qrms8Zr4_M-MbKaVUaiItkosLNPRKS4BVF4uuk8eR26s20_zDnrTQnrSy1HZ2NsShQfL0FI36itl1lzipnMIr--cZJwhvcmB1Im-bTuFGu-Nrk3Fn5g9DldxKQzpPQE2cEuJfJfPKAp9tFDyuHh1jzZULMFaerdiM62a1i01hXOleYFnl70eCGW_0YWtUipPfTzBO8rAdCpCgb76xdehvDlVjeHF0VFvvund3SWXyMBFAP_g9_6Pq2A5NuysCzD97ifb_KDjPDq4dK9XwWR6sqjX-jvA4hrCrRJcpnv2VvblVmAID1fBKx2oisDSdgGLaVX5f4W7XZHyLY-EIwk5PvqsHcz3pZFF52TqWTcMrIH_f-w86dx1VvffJkrb2u5HoCMj6Z9m3JKuCZrIx1ZF1RldvIC8IPEmFQlMfLMEJsgrM03equw%3D%3D)
I'm sick and tired of performative politicians in Washington crowing about cutting waste but not making any headway. That's why I'm stepping up to codify President Trump's Executive Orders and gut the waste and fraud that Elon found.
I'm spittin' mad knowing that government bureaucrats are sending YOUR tax dollars overseas to fund terrorists in far-flung countries. That's about as smart as chickens running KFC!
I'm sure you read about the Somali criminals allegedly stealing billions of dollars using fraudulent daycares...
This job is too big for one man, so I'm hoping you'll partner with me. [Will you take my Nationwide DOGE Survey and help me root out any waste and fraud?](http://links.right-red.com/u/click?_t=7f2cc7f35be547959fee68a54bdac0d9&_m=30a831b3ce0d407d85089aecb663f42f&_e=NOcyjFKAQiycVu94xNpbYyrjty7U4AmJHnKAQo_Vdiutq7LbGg4ziXCV3MGSjT-X0oVW7gbFlDG_hrKO6qc7LUWR64m-J8Lpdodswfe4A3-DnEJaUNRVeVVSXqqBu1gGXcq2M1s0dnGx82t7-wge_f4WiV7EoYdVc-qRt2JWLz6Im40a3wjQy_XKTr1PI1dmyR7MQWt5A8mKiWSeebFTz5Unms7svadsHBwHdpLFpIr3uihWho8boVTH2xuHfPoN3yNcubuErJ1Qj6wtyYWTSvp3dI0vWuDCaa9Jny646nCdWgBbdhKYNQ3ek5Dzko676QxQ8qCM1qEKqqOpN19qJIcvDCCpIEDn1KVAqZ4_nb0HqE_Fn_rEVV7u0CLk3avxttDc4VeiCV2evLOYo2tEHHCXmsXLWTQV8Oq023fGaaXfY4qpupNCjF7kP7IgRc2c_mzrZvUnioKnlsNb2MOGXtUNvr4hrR_b5Ra-6w5owsIyg53UWpxTOy9bZ5kw1lfjiBdlGWswpVHdieOWsP5Jsv9bTkDIExfSVCbyOALIc3K1GeNXhCgIG9q1GgaG74yP8FEtowe8C2zPnScxfVG5JetFFb9lqHSJGS9m9gdyydfCK2S1eUoARzFYJv7xcXonhhFHqaDdBD78FDFeyzy8zPCqZaCKWoZdchCPZYtWJwrr0rfnZDqdTV72jJdQTNuhZ9iZXqodKdj6DTfRmTFVQg%3D%3D)

You and I are going to do great things together.
I look forward to hearing what you have to say about waste and fraud - especially now that I'm the new Chairman.
Talk soon,
Tim Burchett Chairman, House DOGE Committee

name: Voter Action Project HQ
subject: This is a terrible SCOTUS decision
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Knocking out Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act is going to reconstitute state legislatures, city councils, judicial districts, and every other type of political boundary in which racial minorities will be cut off from having a voice in their government. Louisiana, Alabama, and other states guilty of racial gerrymandering in the past have already pushed to restart the practice in the courts in the wake of this case.
This decision will silence untold numbers of voters and spiral our fight for voting rights back sixty years. RUSH $35 or more right now to help fight for the values the VRA stood for and defend our fundamental right to vote >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=050326_3&amount=35]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
MJ: Supreme Court Deals a Death Blow to the Voting Rights Act [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=050326_3&amount=25]
Team: The Supreme Court’s six-to-three conservative majority issued a staggering ruling yesterday that essentially kills the remaining protections of the Voting Rights Act , dealing a death blow to the country’s most important civil rights law.
The majority opinion by Justice Samuel Alito in Louisiana v. Callais strikes down the congressional map in Louisiana and in doing so " narrows Section 2 of the VRA to the point of irrelevance, making it nearly impossible to prove that a gerrymandered map violates the right of voters of color ."
The impact shows how partisan this all is. This decision harms only one political party to the direct benefit of the other and return parts of the country to a suppressive era we thought we left behind sixty years ago.
What can we do to respond? It sounds simple, but just get back to work informing voters, reengaging lapsed voters, registering new ones, and turning out everyone we can for the midterms and beyond.
Team: We need everyone's help, now more than ever. Step up with $35 or whatever you can today and help us defend the right to vote >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=050326_3&amount=35]
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=050326_3&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=050326_3&amount=25]
GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=050326_3&amount=50] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=050326_3&amount=100]
GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=050326_3&amount=250] Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=050326_3&amount=35]
Justice Elena Kagan forcefully dissented. “ I dissent because the Court’s decision will set back the foundational right Congress granted of racial equality in electoral opportunity, ” she wrote.
University of Florida political scientist Michael McDonald also pointed out, “ my quick read of Callais decision is that the majority says if a racial community votes consistently with a party, then it is okay to deny them representation because that’s just partisan gerrymandering .”
The consequences are simple to understand and grave for voter participation. The more vengeful partisans make voter participation feel worthless, the less people actually take part. Needless to say, the less people who participate, the less our democracy and our government looks like the people it's supposed to represent.
It was clear long before the MAGA movement that Chief Justice Roberts has seen the removal of the VRA as one of his top goals for his time on the Court. After decades of different cases, yesterday he seemed to finally succeed.
We simply refuse to let our democracy go backwards like this. Will you chip in with $35 or more to stand with us against these partisan efforts to silence voters? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=050326_3&amount=35]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=050326_3&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=050326_3&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax-deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.
Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=evXYbOfD9Tv1hlE67R6RoXmnKQZIer85lDnhWxEKQ4Q4qnb8ZC%2Fmar8u4s2gK1vp%2Bn6y%2FCyVyWyqL8xyX%2F0nffgXTy4erpT5v6MjaCMlDHlujvSReFk6nUNORyq8Zxx2e8yrX0VjhEKpOxwnAX5Yo4QbMvOzGdSgpAcNQeDgv99DqKQQAOfAps2gPaZKN3NTN%2FW7MRAE9Fajwpn05n%2FwgL6qY4lI1aYA9qh73zjcckoxpxFLO8hVjsvHT0WET3%2BdmVTecRvS6McQqKCyEgKOdGvdsnRe%2FL7yqlSjwJyHPhXu5lxEkrmqMrRWtE6JIs7EBhZS5Eds7L4czcsxaymlPQ8qD4qclZwOU8ZtgvUtgKmqqoJUgqzJZoRdLvY9zlLCtYUepPugXRAfeqPtnIE7tm%2FCWgSnBgv%2B7rMLlMgkP7E%3D .

| | Peter,

My name is Katie Britt, and if it’s alright with you, I’d like to share a little about who I am. **I’m not part of the D.C. cocktail circuit, and I never will be._That’s just not who I am._**

In 2022, I was elected to represent the great state of Alabama in the United States Senate. I became the first woman in Alabama’s history to hold this seat—but that’s not why I ran.

I ran because I’m a _**mom**_ , a _**wife**_ , and a _**conservative**_ who’s fed up with the direction this country is heading. I came to Washington to fight for our families, to protect our values, and to bring some Alabama backbone to a broken system.

I didn’t come here to make friends with the political elite. I came here to stand up for the people who don’t have a voice in the room—and I’m not backing down.
---

I’ve lived a life full of hard work, determination, and breaking barriers. There have been challenges and triumphs alike, but I wouldn’t trade any of it.

My story is a testament to what America stands for—the American Dream. From a small town in Alabama, I rose to become my state’s U.S. Senator—a woman who’s breaking barriers and fighting for conservative values every day.

__**I’m living proof of the limitless opportunity and freedom that define this country.**__

But, I didn’t get here by playing it safe or staying quiet, **and I’m not about to start now.**

The radical Left wants to _silence_ conservatives like me. They want to drown out our voices and push their agenda without opposition, but I’m standing firm.

I’m fighting tooth and nail to keep up and push back hard, for Alabama, for our values, and for every American who believes in freedom.

**The support I’ve received so far means everything, but this fight is far from over.** We need the resources to keep going strong and hold our ground.

**[So, after hearing a bit of my story, will you take a moment to chip in whatever you can afford to help me keep up this fight? The road ahead won’t be easy, but I’ve faced tough battles before. I didn’t run for Senate to sit quietly on the sidelines. I’m a mom, a conservative, and a proud Alabamian—and I came to fight for the values that make America strong.

_**Faith. Family. Freedom.**_

The Left is doing _everything_ they can to tear it all down. But I didn’t come to Washington to play games. I came to fight for the people who feel forgotten, overlooked, and ignored.

From securing the border to protecting our kids and defending our way of life—I’m in this for the long haul.

**This fight isn’t easy, but it’s worth it. And I’m not going anywhere.**

Will you stand with me?

clean_body: From my heart to yours, Peter. | | | | | Peter, My name is Katie Britt, and if it’s alright with you, I’d like to share a little about who I am. I’m not part of the D.C. cocktail circuit, and I never will be.That’s just not who I am. In 2022, I was elected to represent the great state of Alabama in the United States Senate. I became the first woman in Alabama’s history to hold this seat—but that’s not why I ran. I ran because I’m a mom , a wife , and a conservative who’s fed up with the direction this country is heading. I came to Washington to fight for our families, to protect our values, and to bring some Alabama backbone to a broken system. I didn’t come here to make friends with the political elite. I came here to stand up for the people who don’t have a voice in the room—and I’m not backing down. Before coming to the Senate, I served as the first female president and CEO of the Business Council of Alabama, and before that, I was Chief of Staff to Senator Richard Shelby. I’ve never shied away from a challenge—and I’ve never forgotten who I’m fighting for. In every role I’ve held, my mission has been the same: deliver real results for Alabama families and the American people. Before starting my career in public service, I earned my undergraduate and law degrees from the University of Alabama. That experience taught me how to lead, think on my feet, and fight for what I believe in—and it laid the foundation for everything I’ve done since. But long before that, I was just a small-town girl from Enterprise, Alabama. My dad ran the local hardware store. My mom taught dance to almost every kid in town. We didn’t grow up with wealth or political connections. We came from hard work, grit, and doing things the right way.That’s how I was raised, and that’s exactly how I lead. My husband Wesley and our two amazing kids are my rock. In 2011, a tornado tore through our home and nearly took our lives. That moment changed everything, and reminded me what truly matters. Faith , family , and resilience. I’ve lived a life full of hard work, determination, and breaking barriers. There have been challenges and triumphs alike, but I wouldn’t trade any of it. My story is a testament to what America stands for—the American Dream. From a small town in Alabama, I rose to become my state’s U.S. Senator—a woman who’s breaking barriers and fighting for conservative values every day. I’m living proof of the limitless opportunity and freedom that define this country. But, I didn’t get here by playing it safe or staying quiet, and I’m not about to start now. The radical Left wants to silence conservatives like me. They want to drown out our voices and push their agenda without opposition, but I’m standing firm. I’m fighting tooth and nail to keep up and push back hard, for Alabama, for our values, and for every American who believes in freedom. The support I’ve received so far means everything, but this fight is far from over. We need the resources to keep going strong and hold our ground. Thank you, and may God bless America. Katie Britt (R-AL) | | I didn’t run for Senate to sit quietly on the sidelines. I’m a mom, a conservative, and a proud Alabamian—and I came to fight for the values that make America strong. Faith. Family. Freedom. The Left is doing everything they can to tear it all down. But I didn’t come to Washington to play games. I came to fight for the people who feel forgotten, overlooked, and ignored. From securing the border to protecting our kids and defending our way of life—I’m in this for the long haul. This fight isn’t easy, but it’s worth it. And I’m not going anywhere. Will you stand with me? Paid for by Britt for Alabama, Inc.

clean_body: I need your most honest and unfiltered opinion on our administration. The mainstream media is trying to silence your opinions by lies to brainwash the American people. But they don't vote. do. The Radical Left is coming at us with everything they have. To win, we need a reality check from the real Americans who built this movement. you think we need to fight harder, tell me. If you think we are on the right track, tell me. But do not let this email sit in your inbox while the enemy mobilizes. Don't leave us hanging. - Team Rudy |

name: Beth Farnham for Congress
subject: Please Vote for Me to Represent US!
body:
District Round-up 05.03.26 Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you...
---
|
---
| | | [Beth Farnham for Congress](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.com) | [Read on blog](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=https%3A%2F%2Fbethfarnhamforcongress.com%2F2026%2F05%2F03%2Fplease-vote-for-me-to-represent-us%2F%3Fjetpack_skip_subscription_popup&sr=0&signature=aea03e4ff26fc95c2eb71c22a0ae05cd&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z) or [Reader](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.wordpress.com%2Freader%2Fblogs%2F213069660%2Fposts%2F7227&sr=0&signature=fa5516261998a9ba383afa11680b282b&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z)
---|---|---
# [Please Vote for Me to Represent US!](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.com/2026/05/03/please-vote-for-me-to-represent-us/?jetpack_skip_subscription_popup)
---
By **Elizabeth Farnham** on May 3, 2026
---
| **District Round-up 05.03.26**
---
Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here:**[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beth-farnham-for-congress-1](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=8292e16c49b2e6bc59524058500ece4a585dfca38d5acca3857a176202c4b62b&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7227&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=9ba0515e7c9363236a0ec6a27795a9b0&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2JldGgtZmFybmhhbS1mb3ItY29uZ3Jlc3MtMQ=&email_id=5d8c72705e7c783fe39f4352c91321e3)**
---
| **Please Vote for Me!**
---
By now, you should have received your mail-in ballot if you signed up to vote that way. **For all Democrats** – I am on the Democratic ballot to vote for. **For all Republicans** – you are welcome to write my name in and darken the corresponding oval instead of voting for John Joyce. **Please spread the word.**
---
To All Who Made This Ballot Possible – THANK YOU
---
Thank you for giving us a chance at real representation.
---
Thank you for believing in a system of checks and balances.
---
Thank you for forging a path of accountability for a rogue president, his prejudiced administration, racist Supreme Court Justices, and ultra-wealthy Members of Congress who have abandoned their jobs.
---
Thank you for fighting for Reproductive Freedom.
---
Thank you for wanting to establish Affordability of our gas, groceries, and light bills.
---
Thank you for battling for Universal Healthcare.
---
Thank you for caring to feed the hungry.
---
Thank you for defending Public Education.
---
Thank you for demanding to abolish ICE.
---
Thank you for being a steward of the Earth.
---
Thank you for standing with me.
---
Thank you for supporting my candidacy.
---
Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=b121f717cfd63ebe979aaac26fa7e7c0486ccb8a9172c022270adabdfe39b781&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7227&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=e75b831b7ed83965dc6fb4f5e6476b57&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=5d8c72705e7c783fe39f4352c91321e3) !
---
| |
---
| |
---
| | **Endorsements Alert!**
---
Thank you South Central Young Democrats!
---
I am proud to be endorsed by these progressive young people.
---
Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=556ffa1c161476c21ba441950bc3cc098f77dd61821044f484aa524d55dc1bea&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7227&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=ee2378ad096e331fbc6944c679feb9bb&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=5d8c72705e7c783fe39f4352c91321e3) !
---
| |
---
Many thanks to the [Perry County Democrats](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=36aa80dbb50872e3ac83e3564bc891000c9d7ffa96f04a449ea113b01595ff84&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7227&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=fd9a75a23d422cfdf12914aeb7aa8248&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=5d8c72705e7c783fe39f4352c91321e3) for their unanimous endorsement of my campaign. I appreciate all they do for Perry County residents.
---
Their letter reads:
---
On April 19th, the Perry County Democratic Committee unanimously endorsed Beth Farnham for U.S. House of Representatives for Congressional District 13. Beth is a passionate, determined and intelligent person who ran previously in 2024 for this position. She is consistent in her messaging about what the working class has been anxious to hear. Beth will defend public education, vote to raise the minimum wage to a living wage, support and strengthen gun safety laws, reduce the cost of healthcare and will work to reduce the high cost of food and other essentials we need. Most importantly, Beth will work for us, not those who are elected but never “represent” what the people want! We highly recommend voting for Beth Farnham for U.S. House of Representatives for Congressional District 13! She’s one of us!
---
| |
---
Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=b23dc1168aff341eec2acb2d91556f86b0f1f49bdaed269701e4f72c3fcb3086&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7227&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=acca45123a89559aa5b94480bfcdbe7e&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=5d8c72705e7c783fe39f4352c91321e3)
---
| **How You Can Help Bring US Representative John Joyce to Debate Me**
---
Why won’t my opponent, US Representative and multimillionaire, John Joyce, debate me?
---
Because he represents the ultra-wealthy, not Everyday Americans.
---
It’s why he voted for the One Big Beautiful Bill Act – it strips away healthcare from the poorest among us, dramatically increases premiums of Pennie (Affordable Care Act in Pennsylvania), and forces cuts to Medicare.
---
By extending the 2017 tax cuts to the ultra-wealthy, the national deficit will skyrocket, mandating at least $418 billion dollars to be cut from Medicare, forcing senior citizens to pay more out-of-pocket.
---
He doesn’t care that Trump’s never-ending war is costing Americans $1 billion/day.
---
He doesn’t care that Trump puts our national security at risk with decreased weapons.
---
He doesn’t care about rising prices of gasoline, groceries, or light bills.
---
Joyce doesn’t care that his vote for the One Big Beautiful Bill Act strips food assistance from his hungriest constituents.
---
He doesn’t care that his vote for the Save America Act strips voting rights primarily from women.
---
He doesn’t care that mothers needing life-saving D&C’s, mislabled “abortion” in several states are denied them after miscarriage, only to die of sepsis later.
---
The moment he opens his mouth to defend his awful positions is the moment he bares his ugly, tortured soul.
---
And then, you’ll realize that he never cared about you.
---
So he’ll stay hidden, in the shadows, avoiding in-person Town Halls, forgoing fora, and dodging debates.
---
But you can change this.
---
Call upon your county Chamber of Commerce, instutitions of higher learning, newspapers, and television stations.
---
Demand to hear your candidates for US Congress debate their postions at the venue of their choice.
---
And require from them that even if one candidate, the cowardly John Joyce, doesn’t show up, the host will still ask the important questions of the present candidate, i.e. me, Beth Farnham.
---
Because you choose Democratic norms.
---
You choose Transparency.
---
You choose Real Representation.
---
You [#ChooseDemocracy](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=061fe2dce8f5726215f5e2829ff45b7365b56e8ac9d0a4c8ba2a8e00fa73a6a9&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7227&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=8319e9362d000fdabb3582dbdfffa949&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=5d8c72705e7c783fe39f4352c91321e3)
---
| |
---
| **Candidate Forum #1**
---
Many thanks to the Franklin County Democratic Committee for hosting the Candidate Forum on Wednesday evening.
---
Thank you also to everyone who attended.
---
There was much interest on candidate positions regarding AI Data Centers and I.C.E.
---
Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=d1b3595dbe2408ff60f2f862138837254b5ca570c9000088aa7a3b531df693f5&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7227&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=0199f1f3b396596ec2697d0c0bb99978&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=5d8c72705e7c783fe39f4352c91321e3) and Community!
---
(Photo credits: Jack Stansbury and Christina Palesky)
---
| |
---
| | |
---|---|---
| **Supporting Local Businesses**
---
Wednesday afternoon, the Cinco de Mayo meet and greet at the Volvo in Shippensburg was the place to be!
---
Hosted by the Greater Chambersburg Chamber of Commerce, owners and workers alike met to discuss the issues near and dear to their hearts.
---
I met a number of voters including Mike Davidson and also ran into Commissioner Bob Ziobrowski!
---
Small businesses are one of the heartbeats of America but they are especially affected by healthcare costs which is why I’m proud to tell them that I support a national healthcare plan as well as The Rule of Two.
---
Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=67014f02ea328845fa9b72fc0199106e2d1ce525e166ff3cbee9828ee8c982c9&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7227&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=213a810d3e1236074603b57cf4fe00d4&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=5d8c72705e7c783fe39f4352c91321e3) and entrepreneurship!
---
| |
---
With Mike Davis of Rohrer
---
|
With Franklin County Commissioner Bob Z|
Great crowd|
Great crowd from other direction
---|---|---
| **Candidate Forum #2**
---
Many thanks to the Blair County Democratic Committee for, along with the Blair County Young Democrats, chair Owen Smith Meyer, for hosting a great candidate forum at the AFL-CIO Union Building in Altoona on Friday afternoon. It was a great session of candidate passion and voter engagement. I was happy to participate with candidates Caleb McCoy for PA State 79th District, Sarah Holland for PA State 80th District, and Sean Steeg PA Senate 30th District.
---
Together, we Choose Democracy!
---
| |
---
| |
---
Photo credits: Gillian Katzer
---
| **Spring Brunch** **and Doorknocking**
---
Many thanks to the Cambria County Democratic Committee for inviting me to speak at their Spring Brunch on Saturday at the Parkway Diner in Portage. It was refreshing to reconnect with such dedicated Democrats in this pivotal election year. Later, I went doorknocking in Portage and connected with several more voters.
---
2026 is our year!
---
Together, we Choose Democracy!
---
| |
---
| |
---
With Eugene Depasquale at the Cambria Dems brunch.
---
| **Charity of the Week!**
---
Please support The Arc of Cumberland & Perry Counties (CPARC) , if you can.
---
Their mission is to “...empower, inspire, and educate people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, their families, and the community.”
---
Besides services like housing, adult daycare, Advocacy, and education, CPARC also partners with The Sassy Gourmet which employs people with disabilities.
---
Their website is here: [https://cparc.org/support-us/](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=c002101f20f5f638bdf146d16c6cbd4366b17f66afb84ea1b9f5ceb1cf5a7693&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7227&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=182a051a933065deb5281987ba733bcf&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9jcGFyYy5vcmcvc3VwcG9ydC11cy8&email_id=5d8c72705e7c783fe39f4352c91321e3)
---
Check out the menu from The Sassy Gourmet!
---
| |
---
| |
---
| **Comparing and Contrasting Votes on Legislation**
---
Check out how Joyce recently voted, how I would have voted in comparison, and why!
---
---
---
| | If you’re like me, you love this country as much as I do and work to make a positive difference. We don’t just #ChooseDemocracy, we choose Pennsylvania, we choose The United States of America, we choose Humanity and I am deeply glad to work along side you. Sincerely, Beth

clean_body: District Round-up 05.03.26 Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you... | | | | | or ---|---|--- By Elizabeth Farnham on May 3, 2026 | District Round-up 05.03.26 Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: | Please Vote for Me! By now, you should have received your mail-in ballot if you signed up to vote that way. For all Democrats – I am on the Democratic ballot to vote for. For all Republicans – you are welcome to write my name in and darken the corresponding oval instead of voting for John Joyce. Please spread the word. To All Who Made This Ballot Possible – THANK YOU Thank you for giving us a chance at real representation. Thank you for believing in a system of checks and balances. Thank you for forging a path of accountability for a rogue president, his prejudiced administration, racist Supreme Court Justices, and ultra-wealthy Members of Congress who have abandoned their jobs. Thank you for fighting for Reproductive Freedom. Thank you for wanting to establish Affordability of our gas, groceries, and light bills. Thank you for battling for Universal Healthcare. Thank you for caring to feed the hungry. Thank you for defending Public Education. Thank you for demanding to abolish ICE. Thank you for being a steward of the Earth. Thank you for standing with me. Thank you for supporting my candidacy. Together, we ! | | | | | | Endorsements Alert! Thank you South Central Young Democrats! I am proud to be endorsed by these progressive young people. Together, we ! | | Many thanks to the for their unanimous endorsement of my campaign. I appreciate all they do for Perry County residents. Their letter reads: On April 19th, the Perry County Democratic Committee unanimously endorsed Beth Farnham for U.S. House of Representatives for Congressional District 13. Beth is a passionate, determined and intelligent person who ran previously in 2024 for this position. She is consistent in her messaging about what the working class has been anxious to hear. Beth will defend public education, vote to raise the minimum wage to a living wage, support and strengthen gun safety laws, reduce the cost of healthcare and will work to reduce the high cost of food and other essentials we need. Most importantly, Beth will work for us, not those who are elected but never “represent” what the people want! We highly recommend voting for Beth Farnham for U.S. House of Representatives for Congressional District 13! She’s one of us! | | Together, we | How You Can Help Bring US Representative John Joyce to Debate Me Why won’t my opponent, US Representative and multimillionaire, John Joyce, debate me? Because he represents the ultra-wealthy, not Everyday Americans. It’s why he voted for the One Big Beautiful Bill Act – it strips away healthcare from the poorest among us, dramatically increases premiums of Pennie (Affordable Care Act in Pennsylvania), and forces cuts to Medicare. By extending the 2017 tax cuts to the ultra-wealthy, the national deficit will skyrocket, mandating at least $418 billion dollars to be cut from Medicare, forcing senior citizens to pay more out-of-pocket. He doesn’t care that Trump’s never-ending war is costing Americans $1 billion/day. He doesn’t care that Trump puts our national security at risk with decreased weapons. He doesn’t care about rising prices of gasoline, groceries, or light bills. Joyce doesn’t care that his vote for the One Big Beautiful Bill Act strips food assistance from his hungriest constituents. He doesn’t care that his vote for the Save America Act strips voting rights primarily from women. He doesn’t care that mothers needing life-saving D&C’s, mislabled “abortion” in several states are denied them after miscarriage, only to die of sepsis later. The moment he opens his mouth to defend his awful positions is the moment he bares his ugly, tortured soul. And then, you’ll realize that he never cared about you. So he’ll stay hidden, in the shadows, avoiding in-person Town Halls, forgoing fora, and dodging debates. But you can change this. Call upon your county Chamber of Commerce, instutitions of higher learning, newspapers, and television stations. Demand to hear your candidates for US Congress debate their postions at the venue of their choice. And require from them that even if one candidate, the cowardly John Joyce, doesn’t show up, the host will still ask the important questions of the present candidate, i.e. me, Beth Farnham. Because you choose Democratic norms. You choose Transparency. You choose Real Representation. You | | | Candidate Forum #1 Many thanks to the Franklin County Democratic Committee for hosting the Candidate Forum on Wednesday evening. Thank you also to everyone who attended. There was much interest on candidate positions regarding AI Data Centers and I.C.E. Together, we and Community! (Photo credits: Jack Stansbury and Christina Palesky) | | | | | ---|---|--- | Supporting Local Businesses Wednesday afternoon, the Cinco de Mayo meet and greet at the Volvo in Shippensburg was the place to be! Hosted by the Greater Chambersburg Chamber of Commerce, owners and workers alike met to discuss the issues near and dear to their hearts. I met a number of voters including Mike Davidson and also ran into Commissioner Bob Ziobrowski! Small businesses are one of the heartbeats of America but they are especially affected by healthcare costs which is why I’m proud to tell them that I support a national healthcare plan as well as The Rule of Two. Together, we and entrepreneurship! | | With Mike Davis of Rohrer | With Franklin County Commissioner Bob Z| Great crowd| Great crowd from other direction ---|---|--- | Candidate Forum #2 Many thanks to the Blair County Democratic Committee for, along with the Blair County Young Democrats, chair Owen Smith Meyer, for hosting a great candidate forum at the AFL-CIO Union Building in Altoona on Friday afternoon. It was a great session of candidate passion and voter engagement. I was happy to participate with candidates Caleb McCoy for PA State 79th District, Sarah Holland for PA State 80th District, and Sean Steeg PA Senate 30th District. Together, we Choose Democracy! | | | | Photo credits: Gillian Katzer | Spring Brunch and Doorknocking Many thanks to the Cambria County Democratic Committee for inviting me to speak at their Spring Brunch on Saturday at the Parkway Diner in Portage. It was refreshing to reconnect with such dedicated Democrats in this pivotal election year. Later, I went doorknocking in Portage and connected with several more voters. 2026 is our year! Together, we Choose Democracy! | | | | With Eugene Depasquale at the Cambria Dems brunch. | Charity of the Week! Please support The Arc of Cumberland & Perry Counties (CPARC) , if you can. Their mission is to “...empower, inspire, and educate people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, their families, and the community.” Besides services like housing, adult daycare, Advocacy, and education, CPARC also partners with The Sassy Gourmet which employs people with disabilities. Their website is here: Check out the menu from The Sassy Gourmet! | | | | | Comparing and Contrasting Votes on Legislation Check out how Joyce recently voted, how I would have voted in comparison, and why! | | If you’re like me, you love this country as much as I do and work to make a positive difference. In the midst of this Constitutional Crisis, please consider reaching out to your local Democratic committees or Democratic candidates so that we can strengthen our Democracy instead of letting it slide into fascism. We don’t just #ChooseDemocracy, we choose Pennsylvania, we choose The United States of America, we choose Humanity and I am deeply glad to work along side you. Sincerely, Beth | | | | Beth Farnham for Congress © 2026. or . | | | | Get the Jetpack app Subscribe, bookmark, and get real-time notifications – all from one app! | | | | ---|--- | | | | Automattic, Inc. 60 29th St. #343, San Francisco, CA 94110

subject: Tell DOJ to go after Epstein criminals, not "dangerous seashells"
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Investigate Epstein Crimes, Not Seashells](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tcb309d2d-4fb4-4ae9-859c-f2705518bb28/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T04f2da09-f49d-424a-8c70-49dea79dd1d9/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | _(Already did this action?[Go here for others](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tc83a01bb-c32a-48bd-86ca-b84c2848272c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9). Want to unsubscribe? See link below)_ The Department of Justice isn’t doing a damn thing to bring the Epstein criminals to justice, but they are launching a full-on war against former FBI Director James Comey, claiming he threatened the president by taking a picture of seashells (details [here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Taf80c917-c924-4982-95e9-3e94c86e04d4/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)). **[Everyone knows it is bullshit.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T90c6a68e-ca8f-485a-8fa2-fcae2cd6ed7f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Even Trump’s right-wing allies are breaking with him on this.** The DOJ needs to a big 180, shift its priorities and drop this case immediately. And maybe, just maybe, finally go after the Epstein criminals? (after all, it was an island they were on, surely some of them must have taken pictures of the shore, with seashells in them?). **[Sign on, and tell the DOJ: “Investigate Epstein Crimes, Not Seashells”](https://default.salsalabs.org/T86d99c95-6ec2-4ed7-af10-a5925aacbb4b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**

name: alert@cadem.org
subject: We could lose our chance to flip this seat
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5]
Ugh... We need to share some bad news:
A brand-new poll in CA-48 shows Republicans leading the field in both the #1 and #2 spots.
This district is rated “Lean D” and is one we must win in order to retake the House Majority this November.
But now, there’s a real risk Republicans could hold the seat... perhaps even by locking Democrats out of the general election entirely by taking BOTH top spots in June’s primary.
That would be a disaster. And it means we have to act immediately .
The California Democratic Party is already organizing in CA-48 — knocking doors, calling voters, and building the grassroots operation we need to win.
But this poll changes everything.
We need to TURBO-CHARGE our organizing efforts right now to make sure Democrats break through and secure a spot in the general election. Will you rush a donation before our End of Month deadline in 48 hours to help us scale up organizing in CA-48? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-april26?&refcode=&amount=]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-april26?refcode=&amount= [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-april26?refcode=&amount=] CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-april26?&refcode=&amount=]Here’s what your donation will help us do immediately:
➡️ Expand canvassing operations to reach more voters before the primary;
➡️ Increase phonebanking and voter contact to boost Democratic turnout;
➡️ Invest in organizing programs that ensure Democrats aren’t locked out of the general election;
Because here’s the truth: If we don’t act now, Republicans could game the system and shut Democrats out before the real fight even begins.
And if that happens, taking back the House gets that much harder. We can’t afford to let that happen in a district like CA-48... not when the majority could come down to seats just like this one .
We still have time to turn this around. But we have to move quickly. So we’re asking:
Will you chip in right now to help us ramp up organizing in CA-48 and make sure Democrats are on the ballot in November? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-april26?&refcode=&amount=]
CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-april26?&refcode=&amount=]
CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-april26?&refcode=&amount=]

CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-april26?&refcode=&amount=]Thank you for being in this fight. Let's get to work!
- The organizing team at CADEM HQ

DONATE » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=&amount=20.24]
Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action.
If this email was forwarded to you, please sign up for our email list here. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/zWMh-KAhmUiJYxMzOxKADQ2]
We rely on email to communicate with supporters like you and conduct the business of the California Democratic Party, but if you wish to receive only our most important emails, let us know here to sign up for fewer emails. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/60txJAHUg0OznGGaWVwO_A2]

name: Team Katie Porter
subject: This is urgent:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=41253%2E3422442%2E7xh20h)
Peter,
This is the make-or-break stretch for our campaign.
With just one month left until the June primary—and only two candidates advancing to November’s ballot—what we do right now will shape the rest of this race.
**[Can you rush a donation to Katie’s campaign right now to help us reach as many voters as possible ahead of the primary?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260503_3_ak.LJXrs1&refcode=em20260503-41253&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=41253%2E3422442%2E7xh20h)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260503_3_ak.LJXrs1&refcode=em20260503-41253&t=4&akid=41253%2E3422442%2E7xh20h)
Recently, Katie’s taken the debate stage and said what a lot of Californians are living: rents that are out of control, child care costs that don’t add up, and everyday prices squeezing working families at every turn.
She talked about her plans for free child care, tuition-free college degrees, less expensive housing, and eliminating state income tax for families making under $100,000.
The other people on stage—a self-funded billionaire, Big Oil–backed candidates, and Republicans eager to push Trump’s agenda in California—don’t understand what California families are struggling with.
Katie doesn’t take corporate PAC money and she doesn’t self-fund. She counts on people-powered support from folks like you. That’s what keeps this campaign running, Peter.
Grassroots support means we can keep organizing, keep reaching voters, and stay competitive in these final weeks before the primary. Falling short means slowing down at the exact moment we need to be accelerating.
**[So if you can, chip in $5 or whatever makes sense to you to help power this campaign through the finish line.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260503_3_ak.LJXrs1&refcode=em20260503-41253&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=5&akid=41253%2E3422442%2E7xh20h)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260503_3_ak.LJXrs1&refcode=em20260503-41253&t=24&akid=41253%2E3422442%2E7xh20h)
Thank you.
— Team Katie Porter

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20260503-41253&amounts=&t=25&refcode2=41253_3422442_7xh20h&akid=41253%2E3422442%2E7xh20h)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4g-emails?source=41253&t=26&akid=41253%2E3422442%2E7xh20h)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4g-em-update-info-footer?source=41253)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4g-unsubscribe?source=41253&t=27&akid=41253%2E3422442%2E7xh20h)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Governor 2026
PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)
Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
FFPC #1479597

name: Annie Andrews
subject: I'm a pediatrician. I’m pissed. I’m scared.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews]
I'm a pediatrician. I’m pissed. I’m scared. And I need your help to defeat Lindsey Graham in the U.S. Senate.
I’m not the “monsters-under-the-bed” kind of scared. I’m the “I’ve watched children suffer from preventable illness and injury while politicians do absolutely nothing” kind of scared.
I’ve treated kids who’ve been shot. Kids whose families struggle to afford their medications.
Kids who live with mold that causes asthma attacks, miss school for climate disasters, and watch politicians use them as political pawns.
And now? Trump is causing chaos and Lindsey Graham is right by his side.
And Medicaid — the program that keeps half our pediatric patients insured, healthy, and...alive — is on the chopping block to pay for tax breaks for billionaires.
So yeah, I never planned to run for Senate.
But I’m scared. I’m pissed. I’m motivated. And I’m 100% in.
I’m running for Senate in South Carolina to defeat Lindsey Graham and to fight for the kids no one in D.C. seems to be thinking about. So I need you with me.
We need to hit 10,000 donations before midnight to prove that this campaign can go the distance. Can I count on you to split a donation with my campaign and Resist Reclaim Rebuild? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews]
HELP ME DEFEAT LINDSEY GRAHAM! >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews]
I’m running because the Republican Party, led by people like Lindsey Graham, is dismantling the very systems that keep our kids safe here in South Carolina.
I’m running because the issues I care about — children’s healthcare, vaccines, gun violence, public education, Social Security, Medicaid and Medicare — are under direct attack right now.
And I’m running because I consider it my professional duty as a pediatrician — to give children a strong voice at the national level. Too many are comfortable on the sidelines. That won’t work for me.
We need bold leaders who aren’t afraid to fight. I am ready.
Even though the path will be hard, I know Lindsey Graham must be held accountable. He certainly doesn’t deserve a free pass back to D.C. for six more years.
That’s why I’m running for US Senate. To defeat Trump’s lapdog and fight for everyday Americans. But, I need your support today. Can you rush a split donation of $10 (or whatever you can spare) between Resist Reclaim Rebuild and me to become a Founding Donor to my campaign? >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews]
CHIP IN TO STOP MAGA EXTREMISM!>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews]
Thank you for believing in this campaign,
Dr. Annie Andrews
Resist Reclaim Rebuild is an organization committed to helping Democrats win up and down the ballot, nationwide.

1331 FLORIDA AVE NW

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/170c925b-86ca-4bf2-a12e-c087ad05e098?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A few years ago, the United Nations adopted a framework called the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples. It took over 25 years in international forums, far from the kitchen tables of British Columbia (BC), to be drafted. In 2019, however, the provincial legislature in Victoria unanimously [converted the global framework into binding BC law](https://substack.com/redirect/1c78fab3-40b7-41bd-90ee-c026f8c23d4b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Most legislators thought they were making a symbolic statement of good intent. Instead, they were threading a needle through the load-bearing seams of BC's historical legal and economic order.
Last August, [after the longest trial in Canadian history](https://substack.com/redirect/497dd44b-ce41-4ae2-893c-ab8cd1c60732?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a BC Supreme Court judge declared that 3.25 square kilometers of Richmond (the fourth largest municipality in the province and home to almost a quarter million residents) sit atop land that is owned by the Cowichan Tribes. Specifically, the court found the homes, farms, and industrial sites on that land fall under Aboriginal title, deeming that historical and current grants of fee-simple ownership were defective. Stop and read that again. _The court ruled that those who live on the Cowichan land must now worry about whether they own the home they paid for and think they own_.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/421a882c-59cb-468f-b975-29ef556c7cfd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Four months later, the Court of Appeal struck down the province's mineral tenure system, ruling that it failed to incorporate First Nation perspectives. DRIPA's language, the court ruled, was "obligatory and imperative" and mandated that First Nations be consulted. This ruling, known as the Gitxalla decision, has put a chill over the entire sector and introduced paralyzing uncertainty.
Facing a firestorm of concern from everyone, Premier David Eby tried in January to suspend parts of DRIPA to hold the system together. By April, after fierce opposition, he withdrew the proposal. The challenge of addressing First Nation claims is a loose thread of BC society. If the politics pull with enough force, the sweater will unravel.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/22e0a58a-a912-4c4b-a9b4-526a277fe99a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
This is a story about how a global framework, adopted without sufficient democratic deliberation, is now reshaping property rights, investment flows, and the social contract of an entire province. As an outsider who has spoken with several dozen elected officials in Canada, I've come to a simple conclusion: _**The only way out of this mess is forward, through growth, not backward, through litigation**_.
First, we must acknowledge the difficult truth. British Columbia's Indigenous peoples (~5% of the population) were dispossessed of their lands without treaties across most of the territory. The historical injustice is real, documented, and ongoing in its effects. Anyone arguing otherwise isn't engaging with facts. Reconciliation is both a moral and a practical necessity. The question is not whether to reconcile, but how. And I think the current path is misguided. Rather than helping move society forward, it's poised to transfer one community's grievances to another.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..7qppaYrDV8gQj7LUpEgGEjwK_cstAD5KEcRfMDmVqVE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=196373914)
Here's another truth worth accepting. A new legal regime is now allowing a collective claim asserted by 5% of the population to undermine and cloud the fee-simple title of the other 95%. Homeowners in Richmond, for instance, who purchased their properties in good faith decades ago now read newspaper headlines wondering whether their deeds are valid. Mining juniors who staked claims under provincial law now find those claims legally questionable.
The collectivist logic embedded in the UN-inspired framework is that historical group rights can supersede individual property rights. This approach is colliding with a foundational liberal premise that an individual's title, lawfully acquired, is secure. This is not a minor legal dispute. **It is a fundamental question about what kind of society British Columbia is going to be.**
* * *
## [Is Alberta Going to Secede?](https://substack.com/redirect/4b6e6cd8-e6c6-4f39-86fb-76653a50a49e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Jan 25
[](https://substack.com/redirect/4b6e6cd8-e6c6-4f39-86fb-76653a50a49e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Last summer I wrote about a long-simmering movement to have the Canadian province of Alberta secede from the rest of the country. I wrote at the time that the idea wasn't as crazy as it sounds...Alberta accounts for most of Canada's energy resources and production and also has significant mineral and rare earth reserves. The province is teeming with poten...](https://substack.com/redirect/4b6e6cd8-e6c6-4f39-86fb-76653a50a49e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/4b6e6cd8-e6c6-4f39-86fb-76653a50a49e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
In South Africa, an elegant constitutional framework promised both property protection and historical restitution. Three decades on, neither has been delivered. The land-reform process bogged down in proceduralism; promised redistribution was never realized; political legitimacy is now eroding into talk of expropriation without compensation. _**My take: half-measures don 't work and are likely to embitter everyone.**_
In Colombia, the 2016 peace agreement with the FARC ended fifty years of armed conflict. Ten years into a fifteen-year implementation timeline, rural land reform is far behind schedule, more than five hundred former combatants have been killed, and successive governments whiplash between commitment and rollback. _**My take: announcements are easy; sustained institutional follow-through is difficult.**_
While every situation is unique, there seems to be one overarching conclusion I've drawn from my study of these (and other) cases. When reconciliation processes get bogged down in dividing today's economic pie, everyone loses. But When the focus is on the generation of broad-based growth, the politics become manageable.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..7qppaYrDV8gQj7LUpEgGEjwK_cstAD5KEcRfMDmVqVE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=196373914)
Which brings me to the opportunity emerging from today's global uncertainty...and the only strategy that I believe can hold BC's fractious society together. **It 's time to stop fighting over slices and bake a much bigger pie.**
Let's not forget that while British Columbia has been litigating, the world has been re-pricing its demand for goods BC can deliver. Three demand shocks have arrived simultaneously, and each represents a generational opportunity for the province if it moves quickly enough to capture them.
First, let's explore the impact of the Iran war. Since February's strikes and Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz, Asian spot LNG prices have spiked more than 140% above pre-war levels. The IEA has called it "the greatest threat to global energy security in history." Asian importers (Japan, Korea, China, India, etc.) are now urgently seeking what they call "secure" LNG supply. What's "secure"? Cargoes from politically stable democracies, on routes that don't pass through the Persian Gulf. British Columbia's North Coast sits closer to Asian markets than any other major LNG-exporting jurisdiction on Earth. The window to lock in 20-year offtake contracts at premium pricing is now open. It will not stay open forever.
* * *
## [Don't Diversify...Deepen!](https://substack.com/redirect/7a44328c-0236-4545-88bd-817935636fde?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Jan 11
[](https://substack.com/redirect/7a44328c-0236-4545-88bd-817935636fde?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[The key to navigating today's uncertainty, I've argued, is to form a mosaic from the cross-current of the forces buffeting our world. Leaders need to focus on connecting dots across seemingly disparate domains. Siloed thinking around a single discipline (economics, for instance) is a sure-fire way to misinterpret today's chaos and experts who fail to br...](https://substack.com/redirect/7a44328c-0236-4545-88bd-817935636fde?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/7a44328c-0236-4545-88bd-817935636fde?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
Second, the U.S.-China conflict is creating uncertainties. The bipartisan American consensus on critical-minerals decoupling has created a Western premium for non-Chinese supply of copper, nickel, lithium, cobalt, rare earths, and graphite. British Columbia has the geology, the skilled workforce, the deep-water ports, and proximity to the world's largest user of these commodities. What BC does not yet have is a regulatory regime that can permit a mine in less than a decade. Territory-wide consent agreements with First Nations (rather than project-by-project litigation) can help solve this problem and may be single most important economic policy choice for provincial leaders to make.
Third, global demographics are shifting, and that's creating opportunities. The world's population is still growing in absolute terms, while arable land per capita is shrinking. Climate volatility is reducing yields in lower latitudes. Phosphate and potash supply chains have been disrupted by the Strait of Hormuz crisis, since the Persian Gulf moves roughly a third of global urea exports. British Columbia's agricultural land, its Saskatchewan-and-Alberta-adjacent fertilizer logistics, and its Pacific port access for grain exports give it a structural advantage in feeding a world that increasingly struggles to feed itself.
* * *
## [The Race Is On, But Far From Over](https://substack.com/redirect/c724149d-126d-4d14-9d88-dd717a0588cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| November 16, 2025
[](https://substack.com/redirect/c724149d-126d-4d14-9d88-dd717a0588cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Energy, which has usually meant oil, has been a driver of geopolitics for the past 100 years or so. As the internal combustion engine touched every aspect of life, fuel was needed for cars and trucks, industrial machines, and farm equipment. The world's militaries transitioned away from coal-fired power and grew dependent upon gasoline to power tanks, p...](https://substack.com/redirect/c724149d-126d-4d14-9d88-dd717a0588cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/c724149d-126d-4d14-9d88-dd717a0588cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
**These are not small opportunities. They are generational ones that share a common feature: they reward jurisdictions that can make decisions quickly, with capital that flows confidently, on firm legal foundations.**
The path forward in British Columbia must be _**growth-led reconciliation**_ , _**not** **grievance-led redistribution**_. First Nations need to convert from rights-holders-with-veto-power into co-owners-with-skin-in-the-game. The former approach risks holding projects hostage, while the latter moves forward with shared growth. BC should increase the equity-financing program and negotiate territory-wide consent frameworks. And most importantly, BC must restore investor confidence by clarifying--through legislation, not litigation--that individual property rights and collective Indigenous rights can coexist.
The thread has been pulled. The sweater is beginning to unravel. BC can either keep tugging and watch the social fabric of British Columbia unravel as capital flees and grievance compounds... or leaders can together weave something new and stronger. Instead of fighting over slices of a shrinking pie, why not grow the pie? Build an economy large enough that everyone, Indigenous and non-Indigenous, settler and newcomer, gets a bigger share than they would have fighting a zero-sum game.
The world is offering British Columbia an extraordinary opportunity. BC's leaders should be wise enough to take it.

**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. **He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza**. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/ede4fdb7-ceef-4b06-89af-103f75631c12?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/370daff0-4569-4018-9211-05d05a7b0c51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/22ab1339-4d7e-4c98-89c2-e3623ab3ff2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.

What's "secure"? Cargoes from politically stable democracies, on routes that don't pass through the Persian Gulf. British Columbia's North Coast sits closer to Asian markets than any other major LNG-exporting jurisdiction on Earth. The window to lock in 20-year offtake contracts at premium pricing is now open. It will not stay open forever. | | | | ---|---|--- | Jan 11 | Second, the U.S.-China conflict is creating uncertainties. The bipartisan American consensus on critical-minerals decoupling has created a Western premium for non-Chinese supply of copper, nickel, lithium, cobalt, rare earths, and graphite. British Columbia has the geology, the skilled workforce, the deep-water ports, and proximity to the world's largest user of these commodities. What BC does not yet have is a regulatory regime that can permit a mine in less than a decade. Territory-wide consent agreements with First Nations (rather than project-by-project litigation) can help solve this problem and may be single most important economic policy choice for provincial leaders to make. Third, global demographics are shifting, and that's creating opportunities. The world's population is still growing in absolute terms, while arable land per capita is shrinking. Climate volatility is reducing yields in lower latitudes. Phosphate and potash supply chains have been disrupted by the Strait of Hormuz crisis, since the Persian Gulf moves roughly a third of global urea exports. British Columbia's agricultural land, its Saskatchewan-and-Alberta-adjacent fertilizer logistics, and its Pacific port access for grain exports give it a structural advantage in feeding a world that increasingly struggles to feed itself. | | | | ---|---|--- | November 16, 2025 | These are not small opportunities. They are generational ones that share a common feature: they reward jurisdictions that can make decisions quickly, with capital that flows confidently, on firm legal foundations. The path forward in British Columbia must be growth-led reconciliation , not grievance-led redistribution. First Nations need to convert from rights-holders-with-veto-power into co-owners-with-skin-in-the-game. The former approach risks holding projects hostage, while the latter moves forward with shared growth. BC should increase the equity-financing program and negotiate territory-wide consent frameworks. And most importantly, BC must restore investor confidence by clarifying--through legislation, not litigation--that individual property rights and collective Indigenous rights can coexist. The thread has been pulled. The sweater is beginning to unravel. BC can either keep tugging and watch the social fabric of British Columbia unravel as capital flees and grievance compounds... or leaders can together weave something new and stronger. Instead of fighting over slices of a shrinking pie, why not grow the pie? Build an economy large enough that everyone, Indigenous and non-Indigenous, settler and newcomer, gets a bigger share than they would have fighting a zero-sum game. The world is offering British Columbia an extraordinary opportunity. BC's leaders should be wise enough to take it. | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and **. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. Invite your friends and earn rewards If you enjoy Navigating Uncertainty, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe. | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Kelan Publishing, LLC PO BOX 932, Lincoln, NH 03251 ? ?
