name: INGOP
subject: Putting Hoosier Families First
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
Dear Hoosier Republicans, No images? [Click here](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-e-akydtht-hhlljjcji-tt/) _**Putting Hoosier Families First**_ Dear Hoosier Republicans,Hoosiers are seeing real results from the work of our Republican leadership to put working families first. From expanding access to affordable child care, to helping small businesses grow with new technology, to standing up to foreign threats and cracking down on unsafe products, our party is laser-focused on making life better and more affordable for Hoosiers across the state. Our mission remains to keep Indiana strong, safe, and a place where families can get ahead.Now it's your turn to be heard. Early voting is underway, and Election Day is only two days away. Make a plan, show up, and vote, because our state's future depends on Hoosiers like you making your voice heard.As always, thank you for being a part of our team, and please feel free to reach out if there's any way I can be of assistance.Yours in service, Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman _**Governor Braun**_ _**Improves Child Care Access and Affordability, Supports Working Families**_ Governor Mike Braun ceremonially signed HEA 1177, expanding employer-supported child care by extending tax credit eligibility to small businesses and encouraging new local solutions. The law helps more companies offer child care options, increases access for working families, and supports the development of additional facilities across Indiana. Building on broader investments, the measure aims to ease cost pressures, strengthen the workforce, and expand opportunity for Hoosier families."Affordable child care is one of the biggest pressures on family budgets and a big challenge for Hoosier families. By expanding eligibility for Indiana's employer child care tax credit to small businesses, we expand access to reliable, affordable child care and create more options for working parents." \- Governor Braun via a [press release](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-r/) _**Governor Braun Announces IN AI to Grow Jobs and Wages Through Human-Centered AI**_ Governor Mike Braun announced IN AI, a statewide initiative designed to help businesses adopt human-centered artificial intelligence to boost productivity, create jobs, and raise wages. Led by the Indiana Corporate Partnership, the program will connect employers with tools, talent, and technical support to apply AI in practical ways across industries. By focusing on real-world use and reaching businesses of all sizes, IN AI aims to position Indiana as a national leader in AI-driven economic growth while expanding opportunity for Hoosiers."Our goal is clear: grow wages, create more high-quality jobs, and position Indiana for long-term economic growth by making it the most AI-ready state in the nation. _IN AI _is about making sure all of our businesses - from Main Street to large organizations -- have the tools and talent they need to compete and succeed in a changing economy."\- Governor Braun via a [press release](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-y/) _**Governor Braun Strengthens Indiana 's Competitiveness with China, Establishes Safeguards Against Foreign Adversaries**_ Governor Mike Braun signed two bills strengthening protections against foreign influence and tightening oversight of tobacco and vaping products in Indiana. The measures establish new safeguards for state contracts and institutions while giving regulators stronger tools to enforce standards and address unsafe foreign-produced products. Together, the laws aim to enhance security, protect consumers, and keep Indiana competitive in the face of global threats."We are taking action to protect Indiana from foreign adversaries trying to gain influence here at home, and from dangerous foreign-produced vaping products from entering the U.S. market. These laws make sure Indiana is competing from a position of strength and putting Hoosiers first."\- Governor Braun via a [press release](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-j/) [](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-t/) [Subscribe to The Braun Briefing!](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-i/) If you want to stay up to date with everything happening in the Governor's office, don't miss out, subscribe today! _**Chairwoman's County Visits: 35**_ **Shelby County** **Hancock** **County** **Clark** **County** **Allen County** **2026 State Convention** [Click Here for More Information](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-d/) [Confirmed Delegate Registration](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-h/) [Delegate Fees paid by Parties/Organization](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-k/) [Guest (Non-Delegate) Registration](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-u/) Delegates must pay by May 12th! _**Indiana GOP is Hiring Interns for the Spring & Summer**_ **📱 _News You Can Use_ ** **Governor Mike Braun**** **[highlighted](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-o/) his 2026 affordability agenda in stops in Ellettsville and Noblesville to meet with local leaders, discuss community priorities, and highlight efforts to ensure taxpayers are receiving the best service at the lowest cost possible, saying, "My statewide travel allows me to hear from local leaders about the issues facing their communities and to ensure state government is working with local officials to deliver the best value for taxpayers. I want to thank Mayor Chris Jensen for hosting me in Noblesville and the local officials in Ellettsville for their leadership."**Lieutenant Governor Micah Beckwith** [visited](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-b/) with the Club America group at Westfield High School, saying, "These kids really stood out with their thoughtful and engaging questions that reflected real critical thinking. What impressed me the most wasn't just our shared agreements, but also the respectful disagreements. Even the kids who saw things differently approached our conversations with sincerity, curiosity, and good faith."**Secretary Diego Morales **[reminded](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-n/) eligible Hoosiers to make a plan to vote on Tuesday, May 5, 2026. Secretary Morales will be traveling around the state on Primary Election Day, monitoring the election, visiting polling locations and thanking voters and poll workers. Secretary Morales plans to observe elections in several counties and cast his own vote in Marion County.**Attorney General Todd Rokita **[launched](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-p/) IN Fuel Watch, a new public online portal that provides real-time gasoline prices at more than 4,600 Indiana gas stations around the entire state, saying, "We know every penny matters right now and are committed to making sure you are treated fairly at the pump during this time."**Treasurer Daniel Elliott **[addressed](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-x/) the 911 directors from across the state at their annual summit, saying, "Our 911 operators are often our first responders' first responder, talking to people at their worst possible moment. It was important for me to express my gratitude and thanks to our heroes."**Comptroller Elise Nieshalla **[celebrated](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-m/) Governor Braun ceremonially signing _SEA 14_ , authorizing an increase to the employer match for Indiana's Deferred Compensation Retirement Plan, Hoosier START, saying, "When we identified that the match amount was still at $15, the original amount when the benefit started 26 years ago, we got to work finding a budget-neutral way to bring it in line with present-day value, which helps the State maintain a competitive retirement package for employees."**Senator Todd Young**[co-introduced](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-c/)** ** the _Workforce Apprenticeship Growth and Education Support (WAGES) Act_. The legislation would create a refundable payroll tax credit for employers who maintain or participate in a Registered Apprenticeship Program (RAP). The tax credit would offset the cost of wages paid to apprentices and mentors, as well as the cost of other program expenses. **Senator Jim Banks**[introduced](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-q/) the _Promoting Access and Revenue Integrity Through Institutional Transparency (PARITY) Act_. This bill would repeal the 90/10 rule in the _Higher Education Act_ , which only applies to proprietary schools and requires them to derive at least 10 percent of their revenue from non-federal sources, saying, "Right now, the rules single out vocational and career schools while letting other colleges play by a different set of standards. That's not fair when these programs are helping meet critical workforce needs and keeping our economy moving."**Congressman Rudy Yakym**[celebrated](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-a/) the House Ways and Means Committee unanimously passing the _Support for Expectant and Parenting Foster Youth Act_ , the bipartisan legislation authored by Congressman Rudy Yakymand Ranking Member Danny K. Davis to the House Floor. The bill strengthens coordination between the Maternal, Infant, and Early Childhood Home Visiting (MIECHV) and Chafee programs to better serve foster youth who are expecting or raising children of their own.**Congressman Marlin Stutzman **[met](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-f/) with the American Association of Nurse Anesthesiology to discuss increasing their services among rural communities, saying, "I admire their hard work and dedication to providing top care to Hoosiers in need!"**Congressman Jim Baird **[voted](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-z/) in favor of S. Con. Res. 33, the budget resolution to begin the process of fully funding the U.S. Department of Homeland Security, saying, "Hoosiers' safety and the security of our nation are top priorities of mine, which is why I was proud to vote for this resolution to create a vehicle to fully fund the Department of Homeland Security."**Congresswoman Victoria Spartz**[celebrated](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-v/) the passage of her** ** amendment to exempt large farm tractors, combines, and sprayers from unreasonable EPA emissions standards, such as DEF, passing the House on a bipartisan basis, and being included in the final passage of the Farm Bill.**Congressman Jefferson Shreve **[introduced](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-e/) the _Rural Animal Shelter Investment Act_ , bipartisan legislation to help rural animal shelters access existing federal funding to build, expand, or improve their facilities.**Congressman Mark Messmer**[celebrated](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-s/) the passage of the** ** Farm, Food, and National Security Act of 2026 in the House of Representatives, saying, "Our agriculture community richly deserves this much-needed support, and I urge my Senate colleagues to do their part and quickly pass this legislation to get it to the President's desk for his signature. The American farming community has waited long enough."**Congresswoman Erin Houchin**[announced](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-g/) that four Community Project Funding requests totaling over $3,000,000 for Indiana's Ninth District have advanced through the full House Appropriations Committee after previously passing the Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies Subcommittee. **_Upcoming Events_****May 1:[Johnson County Lincoln Day Dinner](https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-l-akydtht-hhlljjcji-w/)****May 2: [Putnam County Lincoln Day clean_body: Dear Hoosier Republicans, No images? Putting Hoosier Families First Dear Hoosier Republicans,Hoosiers are seeing real results from the work of our Republican leadership to put working families first. From expanding access to affordable child care, to helping small businesses grow with new technology, to standing up to foreign threats and cracking down on unsafe products, our party is laser-focused on making life better and more affordable for Hoosiers across the state. Our mission remains to keep Indiana strong, safe, and a place where families can get ahead.Now it's your turn to be heard. Early voting is underway, and Election Day is only two days away. Make a plan, show up, and vote, because our state's future depends on Hoosiers like you making your voice heard.As always, thank you for being a part of our team, and please feel free to reach out if there's any way I can be of assistance.Yours in service, Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman Governor Braun Improves Child Care Access and Affordability, Supports Working Families Governor Mike Braun ceremonially signed HEA 1177, expanding employer-supported child care by extending tax credit eligibility to small businesses and encouraging new local solutions. The law helps more companies offer child care options, increases access for working families, and supports the development of additional facilities across Indiana. Building on broader investments, the measure aims to ease cost pressures, strengthen the workforce, and expand opportunity for Hoosier families."Affordable child care is one of the biggest pressures on family budgets and a big challenge for Hoosier families. By expanding eligibility for Indiana's employer child care tax credit to small businesses, we expand access to reliable, affordable child care and create more options for working parents." \- Governor Braun via a Governor Braun Announces IN AI to Grow Jobs and Wages Through Human-Centered AI Governor Mike Braun announced IN AI, a statewide initiative designed to help businesses adopt human-centered artificial intelligence to boost productivity, create jobs, and raise wages. Led by the Indiana Corporate Partnership, the program will connect employers with tools, talent, and technical support to apply AI in practical ways across industries. By focusing on real-world use and reaching businesses of all sizes, IN AI aims to position Indiana as a national leader in AI-driven economic growth while expanding opportunity for Hoosiers."Our goal is clear: grow wages, create more high-quality jobs, and position Indiana for long-term economic growth by making it the most AI-ready state in the nation. IN AI is about making sure all of our businesses - from Main Street to large organizations -- have the tools and talent they need to compete and succeed in a changing economy."\- Governor Braun via a Governor Braun Strengthens Indiana 's Competitiveness with China, Establishes Safeguards Against Foreign Adversaries Governor Mike Braun signed two bills strengthening protections against foreign influence and tightening oversight of tobacco and vaping products in Indiana. The measures establish new safeguards for state contracts and institutions while giving regulators stronger tools to enforce standards and address unsafe foreign-produced products. Together, the laws aim to enhance security, protect consumers, and keep Indiana competitive in the face of global threats."We are taking action to protect Indiana from foreign adversaries trying to gain influence here at home, and from dangerous foreign-produced vaping products from entering the U.S. market. These laws make sure Indiana is competing from a position of strength and putting Hoosiers first."\- Governor Braun via a If you want to stay up to date with everything happening in the Governor's office, don't miss out, subscribe today! I want to thank Mayor Chris Jensen for hosting me in Noblesville and the local officials in Ellettsville for their leadership."Lieutenant Governor Micah Beckwith with the Club America group at Westfield High School, saying, "These kids really stood out with their thoughtful and engaging questions that reflected real critical thinking. **Good Morning!** On this day in 1979, [Margaret Thatcher](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGunDAQheGnMZ2RGbwYChdp6DZtSjTgYZnIYGRPFvH20V7dN7j1J50j_X-e0xM58vH6jTt5WdN00FUiiVCeJP_bzznjESjMmGemIrifBScw0Gnz0MZOATnek62CX4mA2op845zr-6HphmrzjQ2w9MPgyCwLgMPB2qFdGhc6nNe2q9h_1szD2MaYzri6tz0tHdE8t7A6C8oaCiwp138TH7LRGu_0plwvaa-i30TOotpfCkYF43Vd9cZFUr4_rmCUjYsOeGs-9LcoGJ94a6tg3DG_MJNo2VCWjbIuV8qH5qPKPpwXx8ilc8qa144cvz7lR6HeHv4HAAD__xSMfhE) became Britain's first female prime minister after her Conservative Party won a majority in Parliament. Known as the "Iron Lady," Thatcher served as the U.K.'s prime minister for more than 11 years, the longest premiership of the 20th century. Barbie has had more than 250 careers, and now she's adding another line to the resume: postage. As we report below, USPS is giving the iconic doll a [pink-hued Forever stamp tribute](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO3CAQBuCnwR2r8RiDXbhI4-7SprQGGN8SgXGAW-vePvLmEVJNMdKv__t_fWwfFGI4Pn9S4qXteTv4qpFb47K18pVOW-jw7C0VG7g2SmelDQG1hFGC2jyF-L2pzi87M_LQ8dIbY6Zp7vXcPRdmmNQMgyVtvMNdK9_PoEalfD8OburCcqfBCKoH0GAek5rYaWZrB9yNQqGAfWi5PH7ncLQn7_E7v7g8XE5dXJ6tnVUMPwSuAtfruh7XH_L3U-BKpQUXWeB6u-5DLeRDXrlEL3D9qvLMtVGUlcsrOJb_oNLllDjlQi28WL7dsnITuI5gJA7ODBYmqb13Ulmt5EwjS69mQJz9QLh3ZfHnFWIMVRuh4DNRiO_O7b-Gfi34NwAA__-ftpEG) this summer. Do you own any special stamps? Let us know by replying to this email. Today's sponsor, [Raisin](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1u7CAQhZ8Gd7YA47-C4kord3vblGgMw5oIbAvwWvv2EdEqeYC0842-M3M-7uoOzrvt8R8Cymx3teGVPOaMUeV4hmOJsBk0C8TFYcoQjgSKU97XtKupUAacfylRGWkRObYVSjYMwzhOrJ-qVY4tM2NnYVlop7mAEdikmbUwadCCmcrJYqMdFYzSng7NKEbUPeKytNwOghNB0bi8x-Zzd1te0frX_sTY6D1UXq45H4m0_wifCZ-v62oiuOS2ggmfcavPRPhsI6Y1ZYi5Lnllu53PHFRA485A2tvv54T3haT9jBpJe3sHvscawgHusRXwI1X8TTPGIgOjWBWlOS7nvUv9QAR9BHD---r8p6qfkn8FAAD__-sllWw), helps you earn more on your savings by giving you access to high-yield accounts from over 100 banks, all in one simple, secure platform.

## Project Freedom Begins in Hormuz Today
The U.S. Navy [begins escorting](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOozAQBOCnMZ2R2RgMhYtr6HLtlWiN12FPBiPbCco9_Sl5hb8eaTTf_Lkvd-TIx-M37mRrSMtBV4lUK-Wl5ud-uoyHJ-8wO6ZScT8LLqBgkKqXSi8eOb4X3XgbiIBuDdnOGDOOUzdMzWbNQGbsjVK9D75zRgMEnGDVk8c1qKlh-2lTvdKdUoMy7ahHWgci524QjAahFXmuKbd_Ex91oxDf6UW5XdPeRLvVehZx-yVgFjBf19WurnwUn1zAHPlF8nl6rFQEzJzxkBdm-dXJUjNylSnILeX9-U9yyUhRRnJ4pEOuhIUCZxIwN9n68-IYuQxGaPXYkeN3Rf3RdS8L_wMAAP__zJaFeA) commercial ships out of the Strait of Hormuz today under "Project Freedom," an effort to free neutral vessels that have been stuck since Iran closed the waterway. Trump announced the move Sunday on Truth Social, calling it a "humanitarian gesture" for ships low on food, and warning any interference "will be dealt with forcefully." [Read the post here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2K6zAUBOCnsbqEI1l_LlRcuLjLVgtbmmPpONYiW0ZSEvL2i_cVtp2BD2a-btMNY4r7_QM3cm3J006vmqg1KlMrj-2YC-6BwoxljlQbbkfFSYDQF1AXkFPAmN6TZMEtRIJ6Ro4bY6wduB7Y6vyg0fpFolUqCPTWWB4WDiC96cHMLLpTAwWSA2gwVysteU00z71YjBSdBAqx5XL9znFvKy3pnZ9Urj5vLLm1taN2_b9OjJ0YW3m0tWYfMZ39GUoohOl_3jGFz3NSJ8Yj11Y7MXKuFRdKKS56ZQdugBUXjldMKVZtOgn3DeMvxdqf_nk68RMAAP__hjF3jg). Hours earlier, a bulk carrier [reported being attacked](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkb2OnTAQhZ8GurkyxthQUKSh27QpkX-GZXINRsPcRTdPH7HKG6Q6xXd0pE_n18f84SnT_vnTbzjKUuYdrzOjCPIs_NqOwH5PmILnQHiK347Tz1ppC6oDZebkKb9nU6dxQdTY1jg2zrm-Hxo71OvYtcYmm9A11rtGL4s1rnWmw6UbnOr7msZ7TXXKNEpZ5R696TFaxBBavTijK6MwkRR-_C60y4pLfpcv5EcsW53HVeQ4q_ZHpadKT9d1PWI4-xtWevIsFDNWerq97vBCZYercE43F_HxCWUHYr9Xegqv_ITomQkZ_tFT2JNAWWAtvL3-fHfh8gyv5yYFApPQucJGmcTzG2iPlHAXYDwKS6WnTjmIoXWxCxpS61sw1vcQYujABhsTtkMYgqp5TMdFOdNpXWXU5-Ypf4vKf73zNeq_AQAA__-UAaUV) by small craft off Sirik, Iran, the 24th such incident since the war began. All crew were safe. Trump is also weighing a 14-point [Iranian peace proposal](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOrDAQRE9jMlDTGBsCBz8hm59uiNqmGbwy2DLeQXP7FXOFTSop6ZXqfT3mB_ngj-d_2tmUNc4HX2fgUjjPJf_syWY6Fl4sZev5LLSnk2YEVDX0Nch5IR_es6wWszIjdxWbVms9DGOrxmozklAi9S13vXJsSVnU4zoohtVB37WVNzcNepAtgALdDHJgp5it7XDVEoUEXnyJufmO_igbr-EdX5wbF_cqmK2UdIrun8BJ4HRdV-MO6-5S4HSjBU7Q39EJnD6fap_pqC_KdWJyXKccUzwpNFvZQ5XNki4fgj-VFhKeO_nw2Sp_EvQy-BsAAP__9xl45w) calling for the strait to reopen within 30 days, though he wrote Tehran hasn't "paid a big enough price."
## Trump's Name on Buildings, Currency
The Trump administration has added the president's name or image to [more than a dozen](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2upSAURp8GO04QUbSgmMbuTjul2bC3Hib8GOBcc95-4plHuPVKVr71_fnavsAHn47fEMm0PW-JrhqoNSpbK6942gIJCS0U66k2iGeFTQo5cTFyoTYEH96b6tDsRJKGjkyvtZ7npZ-W7mlAD5PWKJV2C1qwy97jNErcR4VKWdF5c9vEKFQvxCT0Y1YzuYnI2kHuWkmmBKFvuTz-Zp_ak_bwzt9UHi7HLphna2dlwy8mVybX67oeybq74uZMrmcOvnlXmVwxJwjIP11MrjshFQj8uG0pUmocEOt_zhNE4j7CQdy-fECfjsrPko8CsXL3KoWSe_PiEgxqEIvuisHz8iH4OmmmxBHBh8_I9qNnv438FwAA__8G5pD5) federal buildings, programs, and U.S. currency since the beginning of his second term. In December, the [Donald J. Trump U.S. Institute of Peace](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFywyAQheHToM6eZYUWVFCkUee0KTUgVjYZJDSA7fj2GecK6d684iv-r8t8cTHF_frpNrZtzfPOz5q4NS5zK_ft8MXtgYN3xUeuzW1HdTMC0gmGE6g5uJhes-qCXZmR-46t1FobM0oau5v1Qx80aSTo0XuJy2gGCJIceO1Aqy7atwYDKAlAoM9GGV6I2fseV61QKOAQWy7n7xz3duM1vfKDy3nJW5fsrbWjiv5D4CRw-nm_AqfKS7n7mN-zuXavAic5jtSTQZIGNRFJUF2x4XjGlGIlLRRcNxfTH9z-VeNh8TcAAP__VvBwKg) became the first federal building named for a sitting U.S. president, followed by the renamed Donald J. Trump and John F. Kennedy Memorial Center for the Performing Arts. The Treasury also plans to add Trump's signature to future paper currency, alongside a commemorative [24-carat gold coin](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOnDAURb_GdEbmYWwoXIwi0USkSCKlRAY_My-ygbW9g-bvVzO_sN3VLa7uOf-mebIUaN9-2Yim-GPe8coBS8E0l_QZzyXZ3aFbbFoIc7HxzHYGAYqLjgs5O0vhOcvKGY8I2FZoGq113w-NGqq70VI13nUCrZZyEbpvnWrA2qHpYPUdVmRea6ITshFCCV33ssdVIS5LC15LYFKgo3Kk-v9Be7mjD8_jgalej1gFcy_lzKy9MRgZjNd11au39XY8GIz5mQtGBqOngJnBGBEL7RuPtmAiG15dz3-MN94M0-03KK75RHvhb8I_GOmDg_zJoAPx96XjndaDdn4mzPXpfJWMOy8KgbLSTIotWgrvb-VbQh8GvgIAAP__KlqHTw) and a $1 coin tied to America's 250th anniversary. Other branding includes Trump-class battleships, the $1 million Trump gold card, [limited-edition passports](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL_uozAQhJ8GOkdmMYYULq5J97v2SrTGS_DJ_2RvgvL2J_IKV3-j0Xzz52f9QR98ev7GSIb3vCY6WyBmqivXVyy2YnLkLFbrqTHG0nAFCVrISUi1OvThs6remZ0IaOzJDPM8L8t90Pf-MBaGaZv0uCPcrZ0WxMWqYdQDbBrA7b03V5ucpBqk1HK-LWqhTRNZO8I-K-iUJOc519vf7BMftIdPflO9bTn2wRzMpXXjrw4eHTzO87wlu10WF-_gUXLw7LfWwcPlhMGJr9dFKjXvKLHwEZ8kXk0UbK3kyk20V4xURWNkEo4KVo5XFCbJh8CU_Jtqw_oRdUs4Kpj03FfjyulD8E3PnZLPiD58Z_J_ffs28C8AAP__0YCRSQ) featuring his image, Trump Accounts for children, and government websites TrumpIRA.gov and TrumpRx.gov.
## Barbie Stamps Coming This Summer
The U.S. Postal Service unveiled 10 new commemorative Barbie stamps, featuring Mattel's iconic doll in career roles ranging from astronaut and surgeon to paleontologist and music star. [See the Barbie stamps here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOozAQhJ_GdEaLYxtSuLiGLtdeidZ4CT4ZjOxNUN7-xOUR_m6kkWbmmz-P6YExxf35GzdyvORpp7MmYqYycXlthy-4Bwoei49UGbej4qRAWQlGgp4CxvSZdBPcQqTo1pDr-r4fhntn783qjLkvnZ0H03vAeZg1Kgug1DJbYwKFJrorDQzoDsBC3w56oNkSeX9TS6-V0EAhci7t3xx3XmlJn_ym0s55a5JbmY8qbr-EGoUa0ecXt6961MsWarx4Sv5K5Jh3TLJQIqxUhRqvbqFGMNDJL6PkLD3JmRL5gkxBnpFXudMpv_jtyltTXDjOmFKsthcanhvG9H8Q_-jFt1P_AgAA__9lx4di). The stamps will officially [debut July 11](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOozAQBuCnMZ2jYQwYChcnnbjmsu2WyOCBzMo2yHYS5e1XbB5h2xnp1__9n9fpatlz3D5sIFPWfYr0zJ5KoTSVdA_HnGx05GabZqZcbDiynRCwk9BKaCZn2b-mpnJmJUJSFZlaa933Q90N1c1Ab91KCmrVD2RJDdSvQ4cIi11A123F5kyDFpoaoAN96Zuelo5onhWuukHRADkue7p87RzLjVb_2h-ULsseKm9upRxZqD8CR4GjnZdTcP4Ejv-u5_k_PzhuAsd7PrJM5MlmjpusQUZ6yjdNvm0y30OgJHDMZU8voUZ2Qv2tFbadHkBXybjjyd5z7rRoYAuW_U-T8qv5Hga_AwAA__8FwYMa) at the 2026 National Barbie Doll Collectors Convention in Austin, Texas. USPS art director Ethel Kessler designed the set with Mattel, [leaning into "Barbie Pink"](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO3CAQBuCnwR2r8RiDXbhI4-7SprQGGN8SgXGAW-vePvLmEVJNMdKv__t_fWwfFGI4Pn9S4qXteTv4qpFb47K18pVOW-jw7C0VG7g2SmelDQG1hFGC2jyF-L2pzi87M_LQ8dIbY6Zp7vXcPRdmmNQMgyVtvMNdK9_PoEalfD8OburCcqfBCKoH0GAek5rYaWZrB9yNQqGAfWi5PH7ncLQn7_E7v7g8XE5dXJ6tnVUMPwSuAtfruh7XH_L3U-BKpQUXWeB6u-5DLeRDXrlEL3D9qvLMtVGUlcsrOJb_oNLllDjlQi28WL7dsnITuI5gJA7ODBYmqb13Ulmt5EwjS69mQJz9QLh3ZfHnFWIMVRuh4DNRiO_O7b-Gfi34NwAA__-ftpEG) and a rare format, a third taller than typical vertical commemorative stamps. Introduced in 1959, Barbie has [represented more than 250 careers](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L3uozAQBPCnMR1oWRwMhYtr6HLtlWjBC_jkL9lOorz9KZdH-Pej0fzmz329k3U2nL_Js65HXAO_iuNaOa81P3zaMgXDZqO8WS6VfCq0IuDYwq0FuRqy7r3KxuiDGXloWPdKqWma-3FuLg1EKPmYod9hHNUMitU8qV0RTmQmaKz-tMENZA8wguomOfE-Mm_bgIeSKCSwsTXm7m-0oV58uHd8cu726Bunr1pTEcMvgYvApVwxJRvO7k1XjJ-IwOXkwJmcwIVytbvjInB5lFTazI6p2HC2X2D7Fbbl4T3nFlEiDgqwu6p3TdYmvaxztoxKSDg9Wfd_Rf3RdU-N_wIAAP__zd2Dog) over six decades and remains the best-selling doll of all time. clean_body: The Trump administration has added the president's name or image to federal buildings, programs, and U.S. currency since the beginning of his second term. | | | | | | Monday, May 4, 2026 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1979, became Britain's first female prime minister after her Conservative Party won a majority in Parliament. Known as the "Iron Lady," Thatcher served as the U.K.'s prime minister for more than 11 years, the longest premiership of the 20th century. Barbie has had more than 250 careers, and now she's adding another line to the resume: postage. As we report below, USPS is giving the iconic doll a this summer. Do you own any special stamps? Let us know by replying to this email. Today's sponsor, , helps you earn more on your savings by giving you access to high-yield accounts from over 100 banks, all in one simple, secure platform. | Project Freedom Begins in Hormuz Today The U.S. Navy commercial ships out of the Strait of Hormuz today under "Project Freedom," an effort to free neutral vessels that have been stuck since Iran closed the waterway. Trump announced the move Sunday on Truth Social, calling it a "humanitarian gesture" for ships low on food, and warning any interference "will be dealt with forcefully." . Hours earlier, a bulk carrier by small craft off Sirik, Iran, the 24th such incident since the war began. All crew were safe. Trump is also weighing a 14-point calling for the strait to reopen within 30 days, though he wrote Tehran hasn't "paid a big enough price." Trump's Name on Buildings, Currency The Trump administration has added the president's name or image to federal buildings, programs, and U.S. currency since the beginning of his second term. In December, the became the first federal building named for a sitting U.S. president, followed by the renamed Donald J. Trump and John F. Kennedy Memorial Center for the Performing Arts. The Treasury also plans to add Trump's signature to future paper currency, alongside a commemorative and a $1 coin tied to America's 250th anniversary. Other branding includes Trump-class battleships, the $1 million Trump gold card, featuring his image, Trump Accounts for children, and government websites TrumpIRA.gov and TrumpRx.gov. Barbie Stamps Coming This Summer The U.S. Postal Service unveiled 10 new commemorative Barbie stamps, featuring Mattel's iconic doll in career roles ranging from astronaut and surgeon to paleontologist and music star. . The stamps will officially at the 2026 National Barbie Doll Collectors Convention in Austin, Texas. USPS art director Ethel Kessler designed the set with Mattel, and a rare format, a third taller than typical vertical commemorative stamps. Introduced in 1959, Barbie has over six decades and remains the best-selling doll of all time. The new Forever stamps will retail at first-class postage rates. | | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mattdiemer/p/the-picard-quotes-that-built-me?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

I grew up on Star Trek: The Next Generation. I still remember being introduced to it in 1987 when Encounter at Farpoint first aired. My **Unc** was a Star Trek fan from the original series, and he told me there was a new Star Trek coming out and that I needed to watch it. I did, and I was immediately hooked.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8d2ba138-62f4-4289-91aa-5c09b06c76f2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Captain Jean-Luc "The GOAT" Picard
I recorded every episode on VHS. I would sit there stopping and starting the tape so I could cut out the commercials, and I honestly do not think I missed more than a couple episodes during the entire run of the series. My dad kept those tapes for a long time, until my late 30's, until eventually we realized you could just buy the whole series on DVD, download it, or stream it like a normal person. But that process, looking back, was part of the obsession. It was part of the ritual. It was part of loving something before everything was available instantly.
Star Trek is deep in my heart. A lot of people may not know this about me, but I am a big nerd. Science, philosophy, sci-fi, all of it. I did not just watch Star Trek. One of my favorite classes in college was Philosophy and Star Trek, taught by Dr. Kathy Ferguson, who later became my thesis mentor. I wanted to write about Kantian philosophy in Star Trek. Her class was brilliant because she took Hegel, Mill, Kant, and so many others, then showed how those ideas played out inside Star Trek episodes.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f33c65df-20f7-4d22-8f1d-976d7e9c2a80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dr. Ferguson
So when I came across this picture of the ten best Captain Picard quotes from The Next Generation, I kept thinking about it. Not because this is some deep academic breakdown, but because these quotes really did impact me and thousands others. They shaped my personality, my goals, my morals, my ideology, and the way I think about leadership, politics, truth, compromise, failure, and power.
Picard had this code. No compromise, but also enough judgment to know when the rule itself had to be questioned. He believed in sacrifice over self-indulgence. He carried this stoicism that was not always healthy, and in many ways, Picard was a sad lonely man. But for some reason, I admired that too. Maybe because he showed that leadership is not about being happy all the time. Sometimes it is about carrying the weight and still doing the right thing.
**Here are 10 great Captain Picard Quotes and what they mean to me.**
* * *
### 10\. "Let's see what's out there. Engage."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/26cb7628-b97a-4151-bbfe-97f971f9d572?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I am a creature of habit. I love comfort. There is nothing better than sleeping in my own bed, taking a shower in my own bathroom, and living inside the small rituals that make life feel stable. I have never been the traveling hippie type. I know people who can go anywhere, sleep anywhere, camp anywhere, move from place to place, and feel perfectly fine. I know people from the military who can do that without thinking twice. That is not me.
But at the same time, I cannot stay in one place forever. There is too much out there. I need to explore. That is why I moved to Hawaii. That is why I moved to China. That is why I lived in Taiwan. That is why I have been to more than 40 countries and learned foreign languages, because every culture and every language adds another layer to how you understand the world. The more time you spend outside your own bubble, the more depth you get.
And to be perfectly honest, that can be a lonely place. Not everybody has those experiences, and not everybody understands what it does to you. But trust me, the people who do understand find each other.
* * *
### 9\. "There can be no justice so long as laws are absolute. Even life itself is an exercise in exceptions."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef5607f7-fab9-48a7-8d97-ec40474cf081?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I do not even remember the episode this quote came from, but as soon as I read it again, I thought, yeah, this is rebel Matt.
I do not believe laws are absolute. I never have. I am the guy who sees a no right turn on red sign and, if it is clear and nobody is being hurt, I am probably taking the turn. That may sound small, but that is the point. There are rules we follow because they protect people, and there are rules we follow because somebody wrote them down and nobody has questioned them since.
This one hit me because of recent Supreme Court rulings. Laws should be examined. Laws should be challenged. Laws should be changed. And yes, sometimes existing laws need to be broken, because shit, sometimes we have evolved. Sometimes a law was right for one moment, one context, one version of society, and then it becomes the opposite of what we wanted to achieve.
It means justice means change and change means there are no absolutes.
* * *
### 8\. "There are times, sir, when men of good conscience cannot blindly follow orders."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c24e5ed4-1f7b-46c4-ba1e-459b12bb67c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Senator Mark Kelly
This one immediately made me think of Senator Mark Kelly and his statement about troops following orders under President Trump. There are two sides to this, and both are very valid. On one hand, you cannot have a military where everyone just disobeys orders because they personally do not like them. That creates chaos, and there have to be consequences for that.
But at the same time, you cannot have troops blindly following orders if those orders turn them into tools of something immoral, homicidal, genocidal, or unlawful. I am not saying that is what Trump is doing. I **AM** saying there has to be a line.
And this does not only apply to the military. It applies to your boss. It applies to your political party. It applies to your friends. It applies to yourself. At some point, you have to be able to say, no, I am not doing that. The more clearly you draw that line before the moment comes, the more likely you are to actually hold it when it matters. No matter the consequences.
* * *
### 7\. "Let's make sure history never forgets the name... Enterprise."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/19e54b33-cf86-42c6-96b8-d120b431edc8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
NCC 1701 D
Here lies my ego.
I do not know why I am on this earth, and I do not think most people really know either. But I know this. I do not want to die and be forgotten. I do not want to have no impact. I do not want to live a life, fade away, and have nobody remember me two generations from now.
If we are not trying to do something big, then what the hell are we doing? Go big or go home. I know that is not for everybody, and yes, I probably judge people for it more than I should. But I believe we are supposed to ruffle feathers. We are supposed to make waves in still water. I do not want to leave this earth without contributing something positive, something real, something that mattered.
* * *
### 6\. "You cannot explain away a wantonly immoral act because you think that it is connected to some higher purpose."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/75b17671-7b69-4eaa-8583-40a46cb34569?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This one is very relevant because of what we see in politics all the time. Money starts flowing, outside groups start spending on behalf of a candidate, and suddenly everyone wants to pretend the principle changes depending on who benefits. I want to be very clear because people love to twist shit. This is not about one specific candidate. This is about the system. It is about whether we actually believe money in politics is corrupting, or whether we only believe that when the money is helping someone we do not like.
When people say, "Well, you need money to run a race," they are not wrong. You do. But that does not erase the principle. You can still say you are against corporate money in politics. You can still say Super PACs are corrosive. You can still say the system is fucked, even if the system helps someone you like.
You do not get to explain away an immoral act because you think it is connected to some higher purpose, like winning office. Maybe you think your side deserves the exception. Maybe you think the ends justify the means. But does that make it right?
* * *
### 5\. "The first duty of every Starfleet officer is to the truth, whether it's scientific truth or historical truth or personal truth. It's the guiding principle on which Starfleet is based."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/19fe3cf8-f6cd-4a7c-bd8c-e59c60b7e4c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Star Fleet Academy, San Francisco
When I ran for office in 2022, I dealt with personal truth quite a bit. In 2024, I broke out of the consultant-driven version of campaigning and tried to run a campaign that was actually mine. My ideas. My instincts. My voice. My purpose.
Now I feel like I am on a different mission, which is trying to find truth in a broader sense. What do we actually want out of the United States? What do we want out of democracy? How much truth do people really want?
But truth is not just one thing. There is scientific truth, historical truth, political truth, and personal truth. The guiding principle should be figuring out what truth you are actually operating from. If you do not know that, then you are just reacting. You are not thinking. You are not leading. You are just being pushed around by whatever pressure hits you next.
* * *
### 4\. "With the first link, the chain is forged. The first speech censored, the first thought forbidden, the first freedom denied, chains us all irrevocably."
[](https://substack.com/redirect/c7814ed3-8491-4aab-b7c2-59af8a4ac48d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
This might be my favorite quote in all of Star Trek. It may be tied with another one, but this one is personal. This is not just about politics. This is about who you are and the decisions we make and what we allow.
I think about this every time someone in politics says, well, it is just a little compromise. Just take the money. Just look the other way. Just soften the statement. Just make the deal. Just vote for this one thing. Just go along this one time.
But with the first link, the chain is forged.
People do not think one small compromise changes how they legislate, how they represent people, or how they can be manipulated in the future. But it does. That first link matters because once it is attached to you, somebody can pull it. And once somebody can pull it, you are not as free as you were before.
I also think about this with our data, our privacy, AI, online manipulation, corporate control, and every app being designed like a slot machine. We keep acting like the first step does not matter. But it does.
It is like addiction. The danger is not just thinking about heroin. The danger is the first time you do it. That is when the path starts.
So how much compromise do you take? How much do you endure? How many times do you look the other way before you realize the chain is already around your neck?
* * *
### 3\. "It is possible to commit no mistakes and still lose. That is not a weakness; that is life."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/65f4a953-ba36-4b86-bdaa-2dedcc297e83?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is tied for my favorite Picard quote because I think about it all the time. I think about it for myself, but I also think about it for other people. I know people who started businesses and failed. I know people who started businesses, succeeded, and then failed later. I know people who did everything they were supposed to do and still ended up struggling.
And the truth is, some people do reckless things. Some people gamble, cheat, lie, or self-destruct. But most of the people I know who failed were not those people. They were people who tried hard, made decisions, worked their asses off, and still lost. The decisions did not work out, but that does not mean the decisions were stupid. It means life is brutal sometimes.
That is why I believe in a social safety net. Not because everyone who struggles is inadequate. But, sometimes people just lose. And when they do, a decent society does not treat them like garbage for it.
* * *
### 2\. "There. Are. Four. Lights."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4f5cdc4c-5dc5-426f-a735-22eb3e788ff7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is the power quote of power quotes.
If you do not know Star Trek, it may not make sense. But Captain Jean-Luc Picard was captured by the Cardassians, and they tried to break him. They wanted him to say there were five lights when there were only four. It was not really about the lights. It was about breaking his mind, his hope, his spirit, and his ability to trust what he knew was true.
And he did not break.
He said, "There! Are! Four! Lights!"
That means he refused to capitulate. He refused to give in to torture. He refused to say something false just to stop the pain. I honestly think everyone should watch that episode at least once a year, just to remember that whatever you are going through, however hard it gets, do not break.
But I also think this quote hits differently now because of what we consume every day. It is not just about torture in some distant sci-fi prison cell. It is about what people tell us is true, what media tells us is true, what algorithms push in front of us, and what we know in our gut is wrong.
We live in a time where manipulation is everywhere. Some of it comes from inside our own country. Some of it comes from outside of it. Now we have deepfakes, AI-generated bullshit, edited videos, fake screenshots, propaganda farms, and endless online narratives all trying to make people doubt what they saw, what they know, and what they believe.
That is what makes this lesson so important. People can look at the same video, the same event, the same set of facts, and walk away with completely different interpretations. Some of that is human nature, but some of it is intentional. The goal is to wear you down. The goal is to make you so confused, so tired, and so overwhelmed that you eventually give in and say whatever version of reality makes the pressure stop.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/e8999775-f859-4866-94ac-7d069796ef97?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
And that is where the strength comes in. Being grounded in truth, whether that is factual truth, personal truth, or moral truth, is not always popular. It is not always comfortable. Sometimes the easier path is to capitulate. Sometimes the more rewarding path, financially, socially, or politically, is to just say '**there are five lights '** and move on with your life.
But that is the whole point. The strength is seeing the situation clearly, knowing what is true, and refusing to let manipulation pull you away from it. It is not stubbornness for the sake of stubbornness. It is the discipline to say, no, I know what I saw. I know what happened. I know what is true.
**There! Are! Four! Lights!**
* * *
### 1\. "Seize the time, Meribor. Live now. Make now always the most precious time. Now will never come again."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7dff3f66-dcf6-4d0d-98bf-428da127e604?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is probably the most Buddhist thing you could ever hear from Star Trek. Everybody talks about living in the now, but most people do not do it. We live in the future. We live in the past. We predict. We project. We worry. We replay old mistakes. We make decisions today based on imaginary futures that may never happen.
But the truth is, we do not know. We do not know what is coming. We do not know how long we have. We do not know which moment is going to matter until it is gone.
So why not make now the time? Why not live in the now and let it move over you like waves? Do not try to control every second of it. Do not plan so obsessively that you forget to actually experience your life. I think a lot of people would be happier if they stopped trying to manage every possible future and started being present in the one moment they actually have.
* * *
That is what these quotes mean to me. Some political. Some personal. Some probably obvious. Some maybe not that deep. But they are mine. Star Trek shaped me. Picard shaped me. Philosophy shaped me. And whether I am talking about campaigns, corruption, truth, law, compromise, or just trying not to break, I can still hear these lines in the back of my head.
That is what old Star Trek gives you. It gives you a moral question without handing you a clean answer,. That was the beauty of it. It was never on the nose. It was not just an action show. It was not empty entertainment in space uniforms. It was asking real questions about society, power, identity, duty, faith, work, love, marriage, race, gender, sexuality, and what it actually means to be decent when there is no perfect choice in front of you. Or how doing the right thing might actually make you a villain.
That is why I always encourage people to go back and watch the old episodes. Star Trek would put these questions in front of you, let the characters wrestle with them, and then refuse to insult you by pretending there was always one obvious answer. The crew still had to act. Picard still had to make a decision.
And that is the part I miss. That image of Picard staring out the window of his ready room, quietly carrying the choice he just made, is more powerful than most shows yelling their entire moral argument at you from some superiority high horse. It leaves you wondering whether he made the right call, or whether there was ever a right call to begin with.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b3d0df4b-9a1e-43e0-9289-796453dc174f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
That was the beauty of Star Trek. It forced you to wrestle with ethical questions without treating you like a child. It was humbling. It reminded us that you can think hard, act in good faith, do everything right, and still lose.
That is not weakness. That is life.

clean_body: I grew up on Star Trek: The Next Generation. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? We need to find other ANGRY DEMOCRATS. Please consider sharing and subscribing to fuel the ANGER for change. | | | May 4 | | | | | | | | | | | | I grew up on Star Trek: The Next Generation. I still remember being introduced to it in 1987 when Encounter at Farpoint first aired. My Unc was a Star Trek fan from the original series, and he told me there was a new Star Trek coming out and that I needed to watch it. I did, and I was immediately hooked. | | ---|---|--- Captain Jean-Luc "The GOAT" Picard I recorded every episode on VHS. I would sit there stopping and starting the tape so I could cut out the commercials, and I honestly do not think I missed more than a couple episodes during the entire run of the series. My dad kept those tapes for a long time, until my late 30's, until eventually we realized you could just buy the whole series on DVD, download it, or stream it like a normal person. But that process, looking back, was part of the obsession. It was part of the ritual. It was part of loving something before everything was available instantly. Star Trek is deep in my heart. A lot of people may not know this about me, but I am a big nerd. Science, philosophy, sci-fi, all of it. I did not just watch Star Trek. One of my favorite classes in college was Philosophy and Star Trek, taught by Dr. Kathy Ferguson, who later became my thesis mentor. I wanted to write about Kantian philosophy in Star Trek. Her class was brilliant because she took Hegel, Mill, Kant, and so many others, then showed how those ideas played out inside Star Trek episodes. | | ---|---|--- Dr. Ferguson So when I came across this picture of the ten best Captain Picard quotes from The Next Generation, I kept thinking about it. Not because this is some deep academic breakdown, but because these quotes really did impact me and thousands others. They shaped my personality, my goals, my morals, my ideology, and the way I think about leadership, politics, truth, compromise, failure, and power. Picard had this code. No compromise, but also enough judgment to know when the rule itself had to be questioned. He believed in sacrifice over self-indulgence. He carried this stoicism that was not always healthy, and in many ways, Picard was a sad lonely man. But for some reason, I admired that too. Maybe because he showed that leadership is not about being happy all the time. Sometimes it is about carrying the weight and still doing the right thing. Here are 10 great Captain Picard Quotes and what they mean to me. 10\. "Let's see what's out there. Engage." | | ---|---|--- I am a creature of habit. I love comfort. There is nothing better than sleeping in my own bed, taking a shower in my own bathroom, and living inside the small rituals that make life feel stable. I have never been the traveling hippie type. I know people who can go anywhere, sleep anywhere, camp anywhere, move from place to place, and feel perfectly fine. I know people from the military who can do that without thinking twice. That is not me. But at the same time, I cannot stay in one place forever. There is too much out there. I need to explore. That is why I moved to Hawaii. That is why I moved to China. That is why I lived in Taiwan. That is why I have been to more than 40 countries and learned foreign languages, because every culture and every language adds another layer to how you understand the world. The more time you spend outside your own bubble, the more depth you get. And to be perfectly honest, that can be a lonely place. Not everybody has those experiences, and not everybody understands what it does to you. But trust me, the people who do understand find each other. 9\. "There can be no justice so long as laws are absolute. Even life itself is an exercise in exceptions." | | ---|---|--- I do not even remember the episode this quote came from, but as soon as I read it again, I thought, yeah, this is rebel Matt. I do not believe laws are absolute. I never have. I am the guy who sees a no right turn on red sign and, if it is clear and nobody is being hurt, I am probably taking the turn. That may sound small, but that is the point. There are rules we follow because they protect people, and there are rules we follow because somebody wrote them down and nobody has questioned them since. This one hit me because of recent Supreme Court rulings. Laws should be examined. Laws should be challenged. Laws should be changed. And yes, sometimes existing laws need to be broken, because shit, sometimes we have evolved. Sometimes a law was right for one moment, one context, one version of society, and then it becomes the opposite of what we wanted to achieve. It means justice means change and change means there are no absolutes. 8\. "There are times, sir, when men of good conscience cannot blindly follow orders." | | ---|---|--- Senator Mark Kelly This one immediately made me think of Senator Mark Kelly and his statement about troops following orders under President Trump. There are two sides to this, and both are very valid. On one hand, you cannot have a military where everyone just disobeys orders because they personally do not like them. That creates chaos, and there have to be consequences for that. But at the same time, you cannot have troops blindly following orders if those orders turn them into tools of something immoral, homicidal, genocidal, or unlawful. I am not saying that is what Trump is doing. I AM saying there has to be a line. And this does not only apply to the military. It applies to your boss. It applies to your political party. It applies to your friends. It applies to yourself. At some point, you have to be able to say, no, I am not doing that. The more clearly you draw that line before the moment comes, the more likely you are to actually hold it when it matters. No matter the consequences. 7\. "Let's make sure history never forgets the name... Enterprise." | | ---|---|--- NCC 1701 D Here lies my ego. I do not know why I am on this earth, and I do not think most people really know either. But I know this. I do not want to die and be forgotten. I do not want to have no impact. I do not want to live a life, fade away, and have nobody remember me two generations from now. If we are not trying to do something big, then what the hell are we doing? Go big or go home. I know that is not for everybody, and yes, I probably judge people for it more than I should. But I believe we are supposed to ruffle feathers. We are supposed to make waves in still water. I do not want to leave this earth without contributing something positive, something real, something that mattered. 6\. "You cannot explain away a wantonly immoral act because you think that it is connected to some higher purpose." | | ---|---|--- This one is very relevant because of what we see in politics all the time. Money starts flowing, outside groups start spending on behalf of a candidate, and suddenly everyone wants to pretend the principle changes depending on who benefits. I want to be very clear because people love to twist shit. This is not about one specific candidate. This is about the system. It is about whether we actually believe money in politics is corrupting, or whether we only believe that when the money is helping someone we do not like. When people say, "Well, you need money to run a race," they are not wrong. You do. But that does not erase the principle. You can still say you are against corporate money in politics. You can still say Super PACs are corrosive. You can still say the system is fucked, even if the system helps someone you like. You do not get to explain away an immoral act because you think it is connected to some higher purpose, like winning office. Maybe you think your side deserves the exception. Maybe you think the ends justify the means. But does that make it right? 5\. "The first duty of every Starfleet officer is to the truth, whether it's scientific truth or historical truth or personal truth. It's the guiding principle on which Starfleet is based." | | ---|---|--- Star Fleet Academy, San Francisco When I ran for office in 2022, I dealt with personal truth quite a bit. In 2024, I broke out of the consultant-driven version of campaigning and tried to run a campaign that was actually mine. My ideas. My instincts. My voice. My purpose. Now I feel like I am on a different mission, which is trying to find truth in a broader sense. What do we actually want out of the United States? What do we want out of democracy? How much truth do people really want? But truth is not just one thing. There is scientific truth, historical truth, political truth, and personal truth. The guiding principle should be figuring out what truth you are actually operating from. If you do not know that, then you are just reacting. You are not thinking. You are not leading. You are just being pushed around by whatever pressure hits you next. 4\. "With the first link, the chain is forged. The first speech censored, the first thought forbidden, the first freedom denied, chains us all irrevocably." This might be my favorite quote in all of Star Trek. It may be tied with another one, but this one is personal. This is not just about politics. This is about who you are and the decisions we make and what we allow. I think about this every time someone in politics says, well, it is just a little compromise. Just take the money. Just look the other way. Just soften the statement. Just make the deal. Just vote for this one thing. Just go along this one time. But with the first link, the chain is forged. People do not think one small compromise changes how they legislate, how they represent people, or how they can be manipulated in the future. But it does. That first link matters because once it is attached to you, somebody can pull it. And once somebody can pull it, you are not as free as you were before. I also think about this with our data, our privacy, AI, online manipulation, corporate control, and every app being designed like a slot machine. We keep acting like the first step does not matter. But it does. It is like addiction. The danger is not just thinking about heroin. The danger is the first time you do it. That is when the path starts. So how much compromise do you take? How much do you endure? How many times do you look the other way before you realize the chain is already around your neck? 3\. "It is possible to commit no mistakes and still lose. That is not a weakness; that is life." | | ---|---|--- This is tied for my favorite Picard quote because I think about it all the time. I think about it for myself, but I also think about it for other people. I know people who started businesses and failed. I know people who started businesses, succeeded, and then failed later. I know people who did everything they were supposed to do and still ended up struggling. And the truth is, some people do reckless things. Some people gamble, cheat, lie, or self-destruct. But most of the people I know who failed were not those people. They were people who tried hard, made decisions, worked their asses off, and still lost. The decisions did not work out, but that does not mean the decisions were stupid. It means life is brutal sometimes. That is why I believe in a social safety net. Not because everyone who struggles is inadequate. But, sometimes people just lose. And when they do, a decent society does not treat them like garbage for it. 2\. "There. Are. Four. Lights." | | ---|---|--- This is the power quote of power quotes. If you do not know Star Trek, it may not make sense. But Captain Jean-Luc Picard was captured by the Cardassians, and they tried to break him. They wanted him to say there were five lights when there were only four. It was not really about the lights. It was about breaking his mind, his hope, his spirit, and his ability to trust what he knew was true. And he did not break. He said, "There! Are! Four! Lights!" That means he refused to capitulate. He refused to give in to torture. He refused to say something false just to stop the pain. I honestly think everyone should watch that episode at least once a year, just to remember that whatever you are going through, however hard it gets, do not break. But I also think this quote hits differently now because of what we consume every day. It is not just about torture in some distant sci-fi prison cell. It is about what people tell us is true, what media tells us is true, what algorithms push in front of us, and what we know in our gut is wrong. We live in a time where manipulation is everywhere. Some of it comes from inside our own country. Some of it comes from outside of it. Now we have deepfakes, AI-generated bullshit, edited videos, fake screenshots, propaganda farms, and endless online narratives all trying to make people doubt what they saw, what they know, and what they believe. That is what makes this lesson so important. People can look at the same video, the same event, the same set of facts, and walk away with completely different interpretations. Some of that is human nature, but some of it is intentional. The goal is to wear you down. And whether I am talking about campaigns, corruption, truth, law, compromise, or just trying not to break, I can still hear these lines in the back of my head. That is what old Star Trek gives you. It gives you a moral question without handing you a clean answer,. That was the beauty of it. It was never on the nose. It was not just an action show. It was not empty entertainment in space uniforms. It was asking real questions about society, power, identity, duty, faith, work, love, marriage, race, gender, sexuality, and what it actually means to be decent when there is no perfect choice in front of you. Or how doing the right thing might actually make you a villain. That is why I always encourage people to go back and watch the old episodes. Star Trek would put these questions in front of you, let the characters wrestle with them, and then refuse to insult you by pretending there was always one obvious answer. The crew still had to act.
